**[COGNITIVE FOUNDRY, INC.]{.underline}**

**[Enterprise AI Licensing --- Standard Contract Terms Summary]{.underline}**

**ClinIQ™ Platform --- Enterprise Healthcare Deployments**

June 12, 2025

**Prepared by:** Office of Commercial Legal, Cognitive Foundry, Inc.

**Shared:** June 12, 2025

**Recipient:** Meridian Health Systems, Inc.

**Classification:** Confidential --- For Recipient\'s Internal Use Only

*This summary is provided to Meridian Health Systems, Inc. (\"Meridian\" or \"Customer\") for informational purposes in connection with the parties\' ongoing discussions regarding a proposed enterprise license of the ClinIQ™ platform (v4.2). The positions described herein reflect Cognitive Foundry, Inc.\'s (\"Cognitive Foundry\" or the \"Company\") standard contract framework applicable across its enterprise healthcare customer base. These are Cognitive Foundry\'s starting positions; the company has made case-by-case exceptions for strategic accounts, particularly in healthcare, where regulatory requirements may necessitate enhanced audit and data governance terms. Nothing in this summary constitutes a binding offer or commitment. The definitive agreement between the parties, if any, will be set forth in a mutually negotiated and executed Master Services Agreement, together with any applicable Order Forms, Statements of Work, and exhibits thereto. In the event of any conflict between this summary and the terms of a definitive agreement, the definitive agreement shall govern in all respects.*

*Capitalized terms used but not otherwise defined herein shall have the meanings ascribed to them in the context in which they appear or as commonly understood in the enterprise software licensing industry.*

Right-click to update Table of Contents

**[1. Liability Framework]{.underline}**

The following sections describe Cognitive Foundry\'s standard approach to liability allocation across its enterprise healthcare deployments. Cognitive Foundry considers a well-defined and appropriately calibrated liability framework to be foundational to sustainable, long-term partnerships in the healthcare AI space. The Company\'s positions on liability reflect not only its own risk management posture but also the broader ecosystem considerations that arise when a single vendor\'s platform is deployed across dozens of health systems, each with distinct patient populations, clinical workflows, and regulatory obligations.

**1.1 General Liability Cap**

Under Cognitive Foundry\'s standard contract terms, the aggregate liability of each party for all claims arising under or in connection with the agreement --- whether in contract, tort (including negligence), strict liability, or any other legal or equitable theory --- is capped at an amount equal to one times (1×) the trailing twelve-month fees paid or payable by Customer under the agreement. For purposes of this calculation, \"trailing twelve-month fees\" means the total fees actually paid by Customer, together with all fees that have accrued and remain payable, during the twelve (12) month period immediately preceding the event giving rise to the claim, or, if the agreement has been in effect for less than twelve (12) months, the annualized value of the fees paid or payable during the period the agreement has been in effect.

For a contract of the size and scope currently contemplated in connection with the proposed Meridian engagement, the general liability cap would be approximately \$9.6 million, based on an anticipated annual contract value (\"ACV\") of \$9.6 million per year. This figure is derived from Meridian\'s preliminary deployment plan covering fourteen (14) acute care facilities and associated ambulatory sites, and is subject to adjustment based on the final scope of the deployment as set forth in the applicable Order Form.

The general liability cap applies to each party equally and is intended to provide a balanced allocation of risk that is proportionate to the economic value of the agreement. Cognitive Foundry applies this cap consistently across its enterprise customer base and considers it to be appropriate for contracts of this magnitude and nature.

**1.2 Super-Cap Carve-Outs**

Cognitive Foundry recognizes that certain categories of liability warrant heightened exposure beyond the general cap. Accordingly, under Cognitive Foundry\'s standard terms, a \"super-cap\" applies at two times (2×) ACV --- i.e., approximately \$19.2 million based on the anticipated Meridian ACV --- for the following two categories of claims only:

> • **Third-Party Intellectual Property Infringement Indemnification Obligations.** Claims arising under Cognitive Foundry\'s intellectual property indemnification obligations as described in Section 5.1 below, to the extent such claims allege that the ClinIQ™ platform, as provided by Cognitive Foundry and used in accordance with the agreement and applicable documentation, infringes a valid intellectual property right of a third party in the United States.
>
> • **Willful Misconduct or Intentional Breach by Cognitive Foundry.** Claims arising from acts or omissions by Cognitive Foundry that constitute willful misconduct or intentional, material breach of the agreement, as distinguished from ordinary negligence, inadvertent errors, or good-faith performance failures. For the avoidance of doubt, the term \"willful misconduct\" as used herein means conduct undertaken by Cognitive Foundry with actual knowledge that such conduct is wrongful or in reckless disregard of a known obligation under the agreement.

No other categories of liability --- including, without limitation, claims relating to data breach or unauthorized access to protected health information, regulatory penalties or enforcement actions, claims arising from AI-generated clinical outputs, recommendations, or decision-support information, patient harm or adverse clinical outcomes, or any other category of damages, whether direct, indirect, consequential, incidental, special, or punitive --- are subject to the super-cap under Cognitive Foundry\'s standard terms. Such claims remain subject to the general liability cap set forth in Section 1.1 above.

Cognitive Foundry is aware that certain healthcare customers have requested expanded super-cap coverage for data breach and HIPAA-related claims. While Cognitive Foundry has historically maintained that the general cap provides adequate coverage for such claims in light of its robust security infrastructure and compliance program, the Company is open to discussing the specific risk profile of the Meridian deployment in the context of the broader negotiation.

**1.3 Uncapped Liability**

Cognitive Foundry does not offer uncapped liability to any customer under any circumstances. This position is consistent across all of Cognitive Foundry\'s enterprise deployments --- across all verticals, including healthcare, financial services, and government --- and is not subject to exception. The Company\'s Board of Directors and its insurers have determined that uncapped liability exposure in connection with AI-enabled clinical decision support platforms presents an unacceptable and unquantifiable risk that cannot be adequately reserved against or insured.

Requests for uncapped carve-outs --- whether for violations of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996, as amended (\"HIPAA\"), patient-safety claims, gross negligence, willful misconduct, breaches of confidentiality obligations, or otherwise --- fall outside Cognitive Foundry\'s standard contracting authority and would require escalation beyond the Office of Commercial Legal for review by the Company\'s General Counsel and, in certain cases, the Company\'s Audit Committee. Cognitive Foundry wishes to be transparent that such escalation requests have not been approved in the Company\'s history.

Cognitive Foundry believes that the combination of the general liability cap, the super-cap carve-outs, the indemnification framework described in Section 5, and the insurance coverage described in Section 8 collectively provide a commercially reasonable and robust risk allocation framework for both parties.

**[2. Intellectual Property Ownership]{.underline}**

Intellectual property ownership is a foundational element of Cognitive Foundry\'s enterprise licensing model. The Company\'s approach to IP allocation reflects the significant investment --- exceeding \$1.4 billion in aggregate since inception --- that Cognitive Foundry has made in developing, training, validating, and maintaining its proprietary AI models. The following sections describe Cognitive Foundry\'s standard positions on IP ownership and customer output rights.

**2.1 Vendor Ownership of Model IP**

All model intellectual property remains the sole and exclusive property of Cognitive Foundry across all customer deployments, without exception. Cognitive Foundry does not co-own, assign, license (except as expressly described in Section 2.2 below), or otherwise transfer any rights in or to its model intellectual property in connection with any customer engagement. This position applies regardless of whether Customer data, Customer configurations, or Customer-specific clinical workflows are used in connection with the development, fine-tuning, adaptation, or optimization of any aspect of the model.

For purposes of this summary, \"model intellectual property\" encompasses the following categories of proprietary assets, each of which Cognitive Foundry regards as trade secrets of the highest classification:

> • **Model Weights.** This includes base model weights (i.e., the numerical parameters of the trained neural network as established through Cognitive Foundry\'s proprietary pre-training process), as well as any fine-tuned, adapted, distilled, or otherwise modified weights generated through use of Customer data, Customer-specific configurations, or any combination thereof. Cognitive Foundry\'s position is that fine-tuned weights are inextricably intertwined with the base model weights and cannot be meaningfully separated, extracted, or attributed to any single customer\'s data contribution.
>
> • **Model Architectures and Neural Network Designs.** This includes the structural configuration, layer design, attention mechanisms, embedding architectures, tokenization schemes, and all other aspects of the neural network architecture employed by the ClinIQ™ platform, whether or not such architecture incorporates publicly known techniques or is based in whole or in part on published academic research.
>
> • **Training Methodologies, Algorithms, and Optimization Techniques.** This includes the specific methodologies, hyperparameter configurations, data preprocessing pipelines, curriculum learning strategies, reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) protocols, evaluation rubrics, and all other proprietary techniques employed by Cognitive Foundry in the development, training, validation, and ongoing optimization of its models.
>
> • **Fine-Tuned Adaptations.** This includes specialty-specific adaptations, domain-specific modules, clinical pathway optimizations, and other model components developed in connection with a particular customer\'s deployment or a particular clinical use case, regardless of whether such adaptations were developed using Customer data, Cognitive Foundry data, third-party data, or any combination thereof.

Cognitive Foundry\'s position is that model weights, architectures, and fine-tuned adaptations are core trade secrets and proprietary assets that cannot be transferred, assigned, or co-owned. This position is grounded in both legal and practical considerations. From a legal perspective, Cognitive Foundry has invested substantial resources in developing these assets and relies on trade secret protection as a primary means of safeguarding its competitive advantage. From a practical perspective, the interconnected nature of neural network parameters means that disclosing or transferring any component of the model\'s IP could compromise the integrity of the broader model and the interests of all customers who rely on it.

Cognitive Foundry understands that Meridian may have concerns regarding the ownership of adaptations developed using Meridian\'s proprietary clinical data. Cognitive Foundry is prepared to discuss mechanisms --- such as enhanced output licensing terms or preferential access to specialty-specific features --- that may address Meridian\'s commercial interests without altering the underlying IP ownership structure.

**2.2 Customer Output License**

Subject to the terms and conditions of the agreement, Customer receives a non-exclusive, non-transferable, non-sublicensable license to use outputs generated by the ClinIQ™ platform during the term of the agreement (the \"Output License\"). The Output License permits Customer to use such outputs solely for Customer\'s internal clinical, operational, and administrative purposes, including without limitation:

> • Integration of platform outputs into Customer\'s electronic health record (EHR) systems;
>
> • Use of platform outputs in connection with clinical decision-making and care delivery;
>
> • Internal reporting, quality improvement, and population health management activities; and
>
> • Compliance with applicable regulatory requirements, including but not limited to reporting obligations under federal and state healthcare programs.

The Output License does not convey, and shall not be construed as conveying, any rights in or to the underlying model, model weights, training data, model architecture, or any other component of Cognitive Foundry\'s proprietary technology. Customer may not use platform outputs to reverse-engineer, decompile, disassemble, or otherwise attempt to derive the source code, algorithms, model weights, or architecture of the ClinIQ™ platform. Customer may not sublicense, redistribute, or make available platform outputs to any third party, except as reasonably necessary in connection with Customer\'s provision of clinical care to its patients or as required by applicable law.

The Output License terminates automatically upon termination or expiration of the agreement, subject to any applicable wind-down or transition provisions. For the avoidance of doubt, outputs that have been integrated into Customer\'s clinical records prior to termination may be retained by Customer in accordance with applicable medical record retention requirements, but Customer shall not generate new outputs using the ClinIQ™ platform following the effective date of termination.

**[3. Audit Rights]{.underline}**

Cognitive Foundry recognizes that enterprise healthcare customers operate in a highly regulated environment and have legitimate interests in understanding the performance characteristics, fairness profile, and compliance posture of AI systems integrated into clinical workflows. At the same time, Cognitive Foundry must protect its proprietary model assets --- which represent the single largest investment in the Company\'s history --- from unauthorized disclosure, competitive misappropriation, or inadvertent exposure. The audit rights framework described below is designed to balance these competing interests.

**3.1 Annual Summary Audit Report**

Cognitive Foundry provides each enterprise customer with an annual summary audit report (the \"Annual Report\") prepared by Cognitive Foundry\'s internal Model Governance and Assurance team. The Annual Report is delivered to Customer no later than ninety (90) days following the end of each contract year and covers the following areas:

> • **Model Performance Metrics.** Summary statistics regarding the ClinIQ™ platform\'s performance across key clinical domains relevant to Customer\'s deployment, including but not limited to sensitivity, specificity, positive predictive value, negative predictive value, area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUROC), and F1 score, as applicable to the specific use cases deployed.
>
> • **Drift Analysis.** A high-level assessment of model performance drift over the preceding twelve-month period, including identification of any material changes in model behavior, performance degradation trends, and corrective actions taken or planned.
>
> • **Validation Results Summary.** A summary of the results of Cognitive Foundry\'s internal validation activities, including any revalidation performed in connection with model updates, data pipeline changes, or significant shifts in the composition of Customer\'s patient population.
>
> • **Bias and Fairness Assessment.** A summary of Cognitive Foundry\'s analysis of model fairness across protected demographic categories, conducted in accordance with the Company\'s Responsible AI Policy and applicable regulatory guidance.

The Annual Report is provided on an informational basis and is prepared using Cognitive Foundry\'s proprietary evaluation methodologies. No direct customer access to model internals --- including weights, embeddings, intermediate representations, training data, validation datasets, or detailed architecture documentation --- is provided under the standard terms in connection with the Annual Report.

**3.2 Third-Party Audit**

In addition to the Annual Report, Cognitive Foundry will permit a qualified, independent third-party auditor (the \"Auditor\") to conduct a supplemental audit of the ClinIQ™ platform\'s performance, fairness, and compliance characteristics, subject to the following conditions:

> • **Cost Allocation.** The audit is conducted at Customer\'s sole cost and expense, including all fees, expenses, and costs of the Auditor, as well as any incremental costs incurred by Cognitive Foundry in facilitating the audit (including personnel time, facility access, and data preparation). Cognitive Foundry will provide Customer with a good-faith estimate of its incremental facilitation costs in advance of the audit.
>
> • **Pre-Approval of Auditor and Scope.** Cognitive Foundry must pre-approve both the identity of the Auditor and the scope of the audit engagement. Such approval shall not be unreasonably withheld, provided that Cognitive Foundry may decline to approve any Auditor that (a) is a direct competitor of Cognitive Foundry or is affiliated with a direct competitor, (b) has a history of unauthorized disclosure of confidential information, or (c) does not possess qualifications and experience appropriate for the scope of the proposed audit. The scope of the audit must be described in a written audit plan submitted to Cognitive Foundry no later than thirty (30) days prior to the proposed commencement date.
>
> • **Non-Disclosure Agreement.** The Auditor must execute Cognitive Foundry\'s standard non-disclosure agreement (\"NDA\") prior to commencement of any audit activities. The NDA includes provisions restricting the Auditor from disclosing any Cognitive Foundry proprietary information to any party other than Customer, and from retaining copies of any Cognitive Foundry proprietary information following the completion of the audit.
>
> • **No Access to Model Internals.** The Auditor will not be granted access to proprietary model weights, embeddings, intermediate neural network representations, raw training data, or detailed architecture specifications under any circumstances. Cognitive Foundry will provide the Auditor with access to model inputs and outputs (in a controlled, sandboxed environment), performance logs, and such other information as is reasonably necessary for the Auditor to perform the approved audit scope.
>
> • **Permitted Audit Scope.** The scope of the audit is limited to (a) validation of reported performance metrics against independently observed model behavior, (b) bias and fairness assessments conducted using Customer-approved test datasets in a controlled environment, and (c) review of Cognitive Foundry\'s compliance documentation, including its HIPAA compliance program, SOC 2 Type II report, and any applicable regulatory filings.
>
> • **Frequency.** Customer may request no more than one (1) third-party audit per contract year, unless a specific audit is required by a governmental authority or regulatory body with jurisdiction over Customer.
>
> • **Audit Report.** The Auditor\'s written findings shall be provided to both Customer and Cognitive Foundry simultaneously. Cognitive Foundry shall have thirty (30) days to review and provide written comments on the Auditor\'s findings prior to any external disclosure of such findings by Customer.

**3.3 No Direct Access to Model Internals**

For the avoidance of doubt, and notwithstanding any other provision of this summary, neither Customer nor Customer\'s agents, representatives, consultants, auditors, regulators, or any other party acting on Customer\'s behalf shall have direct access to model weights, training datasets, validation datasets, architecture specifications, hyperparameter configurations, or any other materials that Cognitive Foundry classifies as proprietary model internals. Cognitive Foundry regards these materials as its highest-classification trade secrets, subject to the Company\'s most restrictive information security controls, and their disclosure --- even to a trusted third-party auditor under NDA --- would, in Cognitive Foundry\'s judgment, create unacceptable risks of trade secret misappropriation, competitive harm, and model security compromise.

Cognitive Foundry acknowledges that certain emerging state-level AI transparency mandates may impose requirements on healthcare providers and health systems to obtain and disclose information regarding the AI systems deployed in clinical care settings. Cognitive Foundry is actively monitoring the development of such mandates and is prepared to discuss, on a case-by-case basis, supplemental audit or transparency provisions that may be necessary to enable Customer\'s compliance with applicable law while preserving Cognitive Foundry\'s proprietary interests.

**[4. Data Usage Rights]{.underline}**

Data governance is a critical component of both the commercial relationship and the regulatory compliance framework applicable to enterprise healthcare AI deployments. The following sections describe Cognitive Foundry\'s standard positions on the use of data derived from Customer\'s interaction with the ClinIQ™ platform.

**4.1 Aggregated and De-Identified Data**

Under Cognitive Foundry\'s standard terms, Cognitive Foundry retains the right to use aggregated and de-identified data derived from Customer\'s use of the ClinIQ™ platform for the following purposes:

> • **Model Improvement.** Ongoing training, fine-tuning, validation, and optimization of the ClinIQ™ platform and Cognitive Foundry\'s broader suite of AI models.
>
> • **Product Development.** Research, development, and enhancement of new and existing Cognitive Foundry products and services.
>
> • **Benchmarking.** Internal and external benchmarking of model performance, including benchmarking against third-party models, published clinical standards, and regulatory performance thresholds.
>
> • **Research and Publication.** Peer-reviewed and non-peer-reviewed research and publications, provided that no Customer-identifying information is included in any such publication without Customer\'s prior written consent.

De-identification of Customer data is performed in accordance with the methods prescribed by the HIPAA Privacy Rule, specifically either (a) the Safe Harbor method set forth in 45 C.F.R. § 164.514(b)(2), which requires the removal of eighteen (18) specified categories of identifiers and the absence of actual knowledge that the remaining information could be used to identify an individual, or (b) the Expert Determination method set forth in 45 C.F.R. § 164.514(b)(1), which requires a qualified statistical expert to determine that the risk of identifying any individual is very small. Cognitive Foundry employs a dedicated team of data privacy engineers and biostatisticians who oversee the de-identification process and maintain documentation of compliance with applicable HIPAA requirements.

\"Aggregated\" data, as used herein, means data that has been combined with data from multiple sources such that it cannot reasonably be attributed to any individual customer, patient, or clinical encounter. Cognitive Foundry applies aggregation thresholds that are consistent with industry best practices and applicable regulatory guidance.

Cognitive Foundry considers aggregated data usage a critical component of its model improvement pipeline and a high-priority commercial term. The continuous improvement of the ClinIQ™ platform depends on access to diverse, representative clinical data across a broad range of patient populations, clinical settings, and care delivery models. Cognitive Foundry\'s ability to deliver high-quality, continuously improving AI capabilities to its entire customer base --- including Meridian --- is directly dependent on the availability of aggregated data for model training and validation.

**4.2 Opt-Out Mechanism**

Notwithstanding the foregoing, Customer may elect to opt out of aggregated data usage by providing Cognitive Foundry with ninety (90) days\' prior written notice, delivered to Cognitive Foundry\'s designated privacy contact as specified in the agreement. The opt-out is subject to the following conditions and limitations:

> • **Prospective Application Only.** The opt-out applies on a prospective basis only, effective as of the date that is ninety (90) days following Cognitive Foundry\'s receipt of Customer\'s written opt-out notice. Data that has already been incorporated into model training datasets, validation datasets, fine-tuned model weights, derivative models, or aggregated analytics prior to the effective date of the opt-out will not be deleted, extracted, segregated, or otherwise removed from Cognitive Foundry\'s systems.
>
> • **Technical Infeasibility of Retroactive Deletion.** Cognitive Foundry\'s standard position is that retroactive deletion of incorporated data is technically infeasible without materially degrading model performance across the broader customer base. Modern large language models and clinical AI systems do not store discrete data records in an addressable manner; rather, training data is abstracted into model parameters through stochastic gradient descent and related optimization processes. Extracting the influence of a specific customer\'s data from a trained model would require retraining the model from scratch without that data --- a process that would take months, cost millions of dollars, and negatively impact performance for all customers.
>
> • **Impact on Service Quality.** Customer acknowledges that opting out of aggregated data usage may result in reduced model performance, slower model improvement cycles, and the unavailability of certain data-dependent features or capabilities. Cognitive Foundry does not guarantee any specific level of model performance or improvement for customers who have elected to opt out.
>
> • **Pricing Impact.** Cognitive Foundry reserves the right to adjust the pricing applicable to Customer\'s deployment in connection with an opt-out election, as the reduced data contribution may affect the value exchange contemplated by the original pricing structure. Any such pricing adjustment would be discussed with Customer in good faith and reflected in an amendment to the applicable Order Form.

**[5. Indemnification]{.underline}**

The indemnification framework is a key component of the overall risk allocation between the parties. Cognitive Foundry\'s standard indemnification terms are designed to provide meaningful protection for intellectual property claims --- where the vendor is uniquely positioned to assess and mitigate risk --- while preserving appropriate limitations on the vendor\'s exposure for categories of claims that are driven by customer-specific factors such as clinical implementation decisions, data quality, and care delivery practices.

**5.1 Scope of Indemnification**

Cognitive Foundry\'s standard indemnification obligation is limited to third-party claims alleging that the ClinIQ™ platform, as provided by Cognitive Foundry and used by Customer in accordance with the agreement and all applicable documentation (including user guides, integration specifications, and clinical deployment guidance), infringes a valid United States patent, copyright, or trade secret of a third party (each, an \"IP Claim\"). Cognitive Foundry\'s obligation to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Customer and its directors, officers, employees, and agents from and against such IP Claims includes the payment of damages, costs, and reasonable attorneys\' fees finally awarded by a court of competent jurisdiction or agreed to in a Cognitive Foundry-approved settlement.

For the avoidance of doubt, Cognitive Foundry does not provide indemnification under its standard terms for any of the following categories of claims or losses:

> • **Regulatory Enforcement Actions or Penalties.** Claims, penalties, fines, corrective action plans, or other enforcement actions brought by any federal, state, or local governmental authority or regulatory body, including but not limited to the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services Office for Civil Rights (\"OCR\"), the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (\"CMS\"), the Food and Drug Administration (\"FDA\"), or any state attorney general or state health regulatory authority.
>
> • **Claims Arising from AI-Generated Clinical Outputs or Recommendations.** Any claim, demand, action, or proceeding arising from or relating to any clinical output, recommendation, decision-support information, risk score, alert, notification, or other information generated by the ClinIQ™ platform, regardless of whether such output is alleged to be inaccurate, misleading, incomplete, or otherwise deficient.
>
> • **Patient Harm or Adverse Clinical Outcomes.** Any claim alleging physical injury, emotional distress, wrongful death, medical malpractice, failure to diagnose, misdiagnosis, delay in treatment, or any other adverse clinical outcome arising from or relating to the use of the ClinIQ™ platform in connection with patient care.
>
> • **Data Breach, Unauthorized Access, or Security Incidents.** Any claim arising from or relating to a breach of security, unauthorized access to or disclosure of protected health information or other confidential data, ransomware or other cyberattack, or any other information security incident, whether or not attributable to the ClinIQ™ platform or Cognitive Foundry\'s systems.

Cognitive Foundry recognizes that the exclusion of the foregoing categories from the indemnification scope may be a significant negotiation point for Meridian. Cognitive Foundry is prepared to discuss the allocation of risk for these categories of claims in the context of the broader liability framework, including through mechanisms such as enhanced insurance requirements, mutual cooperation obligations, and clearly defined responsibilities for data security and clinical implementation.

**5.2 Defense and Control**

In the event an IP Claim is asserted against Customer, Cognitive Foundry retains sole and exclusive control of the defense and settlement of such claim, including:

> • The selection and engagement of legal counsel to represent Customer and Cognitive Foundry in connection with the defense of the IP Claim;
>
> • The development of the defense strategy, including all decisions regarding motions, discovery, and trial tactics;
>
> • The authority to negotiate and agree to a settlement of the IP Claim without Customer\'s prior consent, provided that any such settlement (a) results in the full and unconditional release of Customer from all liability with respect to the IP Claim, and (b) does not impose any non-monetary obligation, admission of liability, consent decree, or injunctive relief on Customer without Customer\'s prior written consent (such consent not to be unreasonably withheld, conditioned, or delayed).

Customer shall provide Cognitive Foundry with prompt written notice of any IP Claim, shall reasonably cooperate with Cognitive Foundry in the defense of such claim (at Cognitive Foundry\'s expense), and shall not admit liability or make any statement or take any action that may prejudice Cognitive Foundry\'s defense without Cognitive Foundry\'s prior written consent.

In the event Cognitive Foundry determines that the ClinIQ™ platform, or any component thereof, is likely to become the subject of an IP Claim, Cognitive Foundry may, at its sole option and expense: (a) procure for Customer the right to continue using the platform; (b) modify the platform to make it non-infringing; or (c) replace the platform with a functionally equivalent, non-infringing alternative. If none of the foregoing options is commercially practicable, Cognitive Foundry may terminate the agreement and refund to Customer a pro-rata portion of any prepaid fees for the unused remainder of the then-current term.

**5.3 Indemnification Cap**

The aggregate indemnification obligation of Cognitive Foundry under the agreement --- including all costs of defense, settlement amounts, damages awards, and other amounts payable in connection with IP Claims --- is subject to, and not in addition to, the general liability cap described in Section 1.1 above (i.e., one times (1×) the trailing twelve-month fees paid or payable under the agreement). For the avoidance of doubt, indemnification obligations reduce, dollar-for-dollar, the remaining available cap. Amounts paid by Cognitive Foundry in connection with indemnification obligations count against the general liability cap and are not additive to it.

By way of illustration: if the general liability cap applicable to the agreement is \$9.6 million, and Cognitive Foundry incurs \$4.0 million in indemnification costs in connection with an IP Claim, the remaining available cap for all other claims under the agreement would be reduced to \$5.6 million. Cognitive Foundry believes this approach provides an appropriate overall ceiling on exposure while ensuring that meaningful indemnification protection is available for IP Claims.

Notwithstanding the foregoing, as set forth in Section 1.2, indemnification obligations arising from IP Claims are eligible for the super-cap of two times (2×) ACV in the event the general cap is exhausted. In the example above, if the \$9.6 million general cap is fully exhausted, an additional \$9.6 million (up to the super-cap limit of \$19.2 million in aggregate) would be available for IP indemnification claims, subject to the terms and conditions of Section 1.2.

**[6. Service Levels]{.underline}**

Cognitive Foundry is committed to delivering a reliable, high-performance platform experience to its enterprise healthcare customers. The following sections describe the Company\'s standard service level commitments, including platform availability, incident response, AI accuracy reporting, and the service credit framework applicable to uptime SLA breaches.

**6.1 Platform Availability**

Cognitive Foundry commits to a platform availability target of 99.5% uptime, measured on a calendar-month basis. Uptime is defined as the percentage of total minutes in a given calendar month during which the ClinIQ™ platform is available and accessible to Customer\'s authorized users through the standard API endpoints and user interfaces specified in the applicable technical documentation.

The uptime calculation excludes the following categories of downtime:

> • **Scheduled Maintenance Windows.** Planned maintenance activities necessary for platform updates, security patches, infrastructure upgrades, and other routine operational activities. Cognitive Foundry will provide Customer with no less than forty-eight (48) hours\' prior written notice of any scheduled maintenance window, and will use commercially reasonable efforts to schedule maintenance during off-peak hours (defined as 12:00 AM to 6:00 AM Eastern Time on weekdays, or any time on weekends and federal holidays).
>
> • **Force Majeure Events.** Downtime resulting from events beyond Cognitive Foundry\'s reasonable control, including natural disasters, acts of war or terrorism, pandemics, government orders, and widespread internet or telecommunications outages.
>
> • **Customer-Caused Downtime.** Downtime resulting from actions or omissions of Customer or its authorized users, including misconfiguration of Customer\'s systems, unauthorized modifications to the integration environment, or use of the platform in a manner inconsistent with the applicable documentation.
>
> • **Third-Party Dependencies.** Downtime attributable to failures of third-party systems, infrastructure providers, or integration partners that are outside Cognitive Foundry\'s reasonable control, provided that Cognitive Foundry has exercised commercially reasonable due diligence in the selection and management of such third parties.

**6.2 Response-Time Targets**

Incident response-time targets are tiered by severity level and are set forth in detail in the applicable Service Level Exhibit to the agreement. Cognitive Foundry\'s standard response-time framework is organized as follows:

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Severity Level**      **Description**                                                                               **Target Response Time**
  ----------------------- --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --------------------------
  Severity 1 (Critical)   Complete platform outage or critical functionality failure affecting patient care workflows   30 minutes

  Severity 2 (High)       Significant degradation of platform performance or functionality; workaround not available    2 hours

  Severity 3 (Medium)     Moderate impact on platform functionality; workaround available                               8 business hours

  Severity 4 (Low)        Minor issue with minimal impact on operations; cosmetic or documentation-related              2 business days
  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Response-time targets are measured from the time of acknowledgment of the incident by Cognitive Foundry\'s 24/7 Support Operations Center (\"SOC\"), not from the time of Customer\'s initial report. Cognitive Foundry\'s SOC operates continuously, and acknowledgment of Severity 1 and Severity 2 incidents is targeted within fifteen (15) minutes of receipt of the incident report.

Response-time targets represent target commitments, not guaranteed resolution times. Cognitive Foundry will use commercially reasonable efforts to resolve incidents as promptly as practicable following the initial response, and will provide Customer with regular status updates at intervals appropriate to the severity of the incident.

**6.3 AI Accuracy Metrics**

AI model accuracy --- including sensitivity, specificity, positive predictive value, negative predictive value, area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUROC), F1 score, calibration metrics, and related clinical performance metrics --- is reported to Customer on a quarterly basis through the quarterly performance dashboard (the \"Performance Report\"). The Performance Report provides a detailed breakdown of model performance by clinical domain, use case, and patient demographic category, as applicable to Customer\'s deployed configuration.

The Performance Report is provided as an informational metric only. Cognitive Foundry does not offer binding accuracy benchmarks, does not guarantee specific accuracy thresholds, and does not issue service credits, refunds, or other remedies for variations in model accuracy, performance degradation, or any other change in the clinical performance characteristics of the ClinIQ™ platform. Model performance is inherently subject to variability based on a range of factors, many of which are outside Cognitive Foundry\'s control, including but not limited to:

> • The quality, completeness, and consistency of input data provided by Customer\'s EHR and other clinical systems;
>
> • The clinical context in which the platform is deployed, including acuity levels, patient complexity, and clinical specialty;
>
> • Patient population characteristics, including demographic composition, disease prevalence, and comorbidity patterns;
>
> • Integration configuration, including data mapping, terminology alignment, and workflow integration decisions;
>
> • Changes in clinical practice patterns, coding conventions, or documentation standards over time; and
>
> • Regulatory or formulary changes that affect the applicability of model recommendations.

Cognitive Foundry\'s obligation with respect to model accuracy is limited to commercially reasonable efforts to maintain and improve model performance over time through ongoing monitoring, retraining, validation, and optimization activities conducted in accordance with the Company\'s model lifecycle management program.

**6.4 Service Credits for Uptime SLA Breach**

In the event of a failure to meet the 99.5% monthly uptime target set forth in Section 6.1, Cognitive Foundry will issue service credits to Customer in accordance with the following schedule, as further detailed in the Service Level Exhibit:

  -------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Monthly Uptime Percentage**       **Service Credit (as % of Monthly Fees)**
  ----------------------------------- -------------------------------------------
  99.0% -- 99.49%                     3%

  98.0% -- 98.99%                     5%

  95.0% -- 97.99%                     7%

  Below 95.0%                         10%
  -------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Service credits for uptime SLA breaches are capped at ten percent (10%) of the applicable monthly fees for the affected month. Service credits are applied as a credit against Customer\'s next invoice and are not redeemable for cash or refund.

To receive a service credit, Customer must submit a written request to Cognitive Foundry within thirty (30) days following the end of the affected month, identifying the specific downtime events and the applicable service credit tier. Cognitive Foundry will verify Customer\'s claim against its own monitoring data and will issue the applicable service credit within fifteen (15) business days of verification.

Service credits constitute Customer\'s sole and exclusive remedy for failures to meet the uptime SLA set forth in Section 6.1. Customer\'s right to receive service credits is in addition to, and does not limit, Customer\'s termination rights under Section 7 in the event of a material, uncured breach of the agreement.

**[7. Term and Termination]{.underline}**

**7.1 Term.** The initial term of the agreement shall be as set forth in the applicable Order Form executed by both parties. For the proposed Meridian engagement, the anticipated initial term is three (3) years from the effective date of the agreement, though the final term length remains subject to negotiation and mutual agreement.

The agreement shall automatically renew for successive renewal terms of one (1) year each at the end of the initial term, unless either party provides written notice of non-renewal at least one hundred twenty (120) days prior to the end of the then-current term. Renewal-term pricing is subject to adjustment by Cognitive Foundry in accordance with the pricing escalation provisions set forth in the applicable Order Form, provided that any such adjustment shall not exceed the greater of (a) three percent (3%) of the prior-year fees or (b) the percentage increase in the Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) published by the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics for the twelve-month period ending on the most recent publication date prior to the renewal date.

Termination during the initial term requires mutual written consent of both parties, except in the case of a material uncured breach by either party, as further specified in the agreement. A party asserting a right to terminate for material breach must provide the breaching party with written notice specifying the nature of the breach in reasonable detail and allow the breaching party a cure period of not less than thirty (30) days (or such longer period as may be reasonable under the circumstances if the breach is not reasonably susceptible to cure within thirty (30) days, provided the breaching party has commenced good-faith efforts to cure within such period and is diligently pursuing such efforts).

**7.2 Early Termination by Customer**

In the event Customer elects to terminate the agreement prior to the expiration of the initial term for any reason other than Cognitive Foundry\'s material uncured breach (a \"Customer Convenience Termination\"), Customer shall pay an early termination fee (the \"ETF\") calculated as follows:

> • The ETF shall be equal to seventy-five percent (75%) of the remaining contract value, defined as the aggregate of all fees that would have been payable by Customer through the end of the then-current initial term, had the agreement remained in effect for its full initial term.
>
> • By way of illustration: if Customer elects to terminate after twelve (12) months of a thirty-six (36) month initial term, and the total contract value for the initial term is \$28.8 million (i.e., \$9.6 million per year × 3 years), the remaining contract value would be \$19.2 million (i.e., the fees attributable to the remaining twenty-four (24) months), and the ETF would be \$14.4 million (i.e., 75% × \$19.2 million).
>
> • The ETF is payable within thirty (30) days of the effective date of termination and is non-refundable under any circumstances. Customer acknowledges that the ETF reflects a reasonable estimate of Cognitive Foundry\'s anticipated losses in connection with an early termination, including but not limited to lost revenue, unrecovered implementation costs, infrastructure commitments, and opportunity costs, and is not a penalty.
>
> • The ETF shall be in addition to all fees accrued and payable through the effective date of termination, which shall remain due and payable in accordance with the applicable payment terms.

Cognitive Foundry\'s inclusion of the ETF in its standard terms reflects the significant upfront investment that Cognitive Foundry makes in onboarding, implementing, and optimizing the ClinIQ™ platform for each enterprise customer, including dedicated engineering resources, clinical validation activities, and integration support that typically spans four to six months.

**7.3 Transition Services**

Upon termination or expiration of the agreement for any reason, Cognitive Foundry will provide transition assistance services (the \"Transition Services\") to facilitate an orderly transition of Customer\'s operations away from the ClinIQ™ platform and, if applicable, to a successor platform or vendor. The Transition Services are subject to the following terms:

> • **Duration.** Transition Services are available for a period of up to twelve (12) months following the effective date of termination or expiration (the \"Transition Period\"). Customer must request Transition Services in writing no later than thirty (30) days following the effective date of termination or expiration.
>
> • **Pricing.** Transition Services are provided at Cognitive Foundry\'s then-current standard professional services rates, plus a transition surcharge of fifteen percent (15%) to reflect the incremental cost and complexity of supporting a customer in transition. Cognitive Foundry\'s current standard professional services rates are available upon request and are subject to annual adjustment.
>
> • **Scope.** Transition Services include the following categories of assistance, subject to Cognitive Foundry\'s intellectual property protections and the confidentiality provisions of the agreement:
>
> \(a\) **Data Export.** Extraction and delivery of Customer\'s data --- including clinical outputs, configuration data, and integration metadata --- in standard, machine-readable formats (e.g., CSV, JSON, HL7 FHIR, or other formats as mutually agreed). Data export does not include Cognitive Foundry\'s proprietary model data, model weights, training data, or any other Cognitive Foundry IP.
>
> \(b\) **Configuration Documentation.** Delivery of documentation describing Customer\'s platform configuration, including clinical workflow settings, alert thresholds, user permissions, and integration parameters, to the extent such documentation exists and is not classified as Cognitive Foundry proprietary information.
>
> \(c\) **Successor Vendor Cooperation.** Reasonable cooperation with a successor vendor identified by Customer, including participation in knowledge transfer sessions and provision of technical information necessary to facilitate the migration, subject to the execution of an appropriate NDA by the successor vendor and Cognitive Foundry\'s right to withhold any information that constitutes Cognitive Foundry IP or trade secrets.
>
> \(d\) **Continued Platform Access.** During the Transition Period, Customer may continue to access and use the ClinIQ™ platform on a limited basis (as mutually agreed) solely for the purpose of facilitating the transition. Continued platform access during the Transition Period is subject to the same terms and conditions as during the operative term of the agreement, including applicable fees (prorated on a monthly basis at the then-current rate, plus the transition surcharge).
>
> • **Intellectual Property Protections.** For the avoidance of doubt, nothing in this Section 7.3 shall be construed to require Cognitive Foundry to disclose, transfer, or make available any Cognitive Foundry IP, including model weights, architectures, training data, or proprietary algorithms. Transition Services are designed to facilitate a smooth operational transition, not to enable the replication of the ClinIQ™ platform or any component thereof.

**[8. Insurance]{.underline}**

**8.1 Current Coverage**

Cognitive Foundry currently maintains a combined cyber liability and technology errors and omissions (\"E&O\") insurance policy that provides coverage for claims arising from data breaches, cybersecurity incidents, technology errors, omissions, and related liabilities. The current coverage parameters are as follows:

  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Coverage Parameter**                    **Detail**
  ----------------------------------------- ----------------------------------------------------------
  **Carrier**                               Northfield Mutual Insurance Co.

  **Policy Number**                         NM-CY-2025-04417

  **Per-Occurrence Limit**                  \$25,000,000

  **Aggregate Limit**                       \$50,000,000

  **Policy Period**                         Current; renewed annually on September 1

  **Coverage Territory**                    Worldwide (subject to applicable sanctions restrictions)

  **Deductible / Self-Insured Retention**   \$500,000 per occurrence
  ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Cognitive Foundry will, upon Customer\'s reasonable request, provide a certificate of insurance evidencing the foregoing coverage. Cognitive Foundry shall maintain the above-described coverage (or coverage with materially equivalent terms and limits) throughout the term of the agreement.

**8.2 Coverage Evaluation**

Cognitive Foundry is actively evaluating an increase to its cyber/E&O coverage limits in connection with its 2026 policy renewal cycle, which is scheduled for September 1, 2026. The evaluation is being conducted in consultation with Cognitive Foundry\'s insurance broker and external risk advisors, and takes into account the growth in the Company\'s enterprise customer base, the increasing complexity of the regulatory landscape, and emerging industry benchmarks for AI vendors serving the healthcare sector.

Any adjustments to coverage levels --- whether increases in per-occurrence limits, aggregate limits, or the addition of new coverage categories --- will be communicated to enterprise customers upon the effective date of the renewed policy. Cognitive Foundry does not commit to specific future coverage thresholds as a contractual obligation at this time, as insurance availability, pricing, and terms are subject to market conditions and underwriting decisions that are outside Cognitive Foundry\'s control.

Cognitive Foundry welcomes the opportunity to discuss Meridian\'s specific insurance requirements and expectations in the context of the broader contract negotiation, and is prepared to consider reasonable contractual commitments regarding minimum insurance levels, provided such commitments are commercially available and reasonably priced.

**[9. General Notes]{.underline}**

This summary reflects Cognitive Foundry\'s standard enterprise licensing positions as maintained across its healthcare customer base, encompassing more than forty (40) active enterprise deployments with health systems and academic medical centers across the United States. The positions described herein represent Cognitive Foundry\'s starting framework and are intended to serve as a foundation for productive negotiations with Meridian.

Cognitive Foundry recognizes that healthcare deployments, including the proposed Meridian engagement, may be subject to heightened regulatory requirements that distinguish them from Cognitive Foundry\'s deployments in other industry verticals. These requirements include, without limitation:

> • The privacy and security requirements of HIPAA, including the HIPAA Privacy Rule, Security Rule, and Breach Notification Rule, as well as the requirements of applicable Business Associate Agreements;
>
> • Emerging state-level AI transparency mandates, including legislation enacted or proposed in California, Colorado, Illinois, New York, and other jurisdictions that may impose disclosure, impact assessment, or audit obligations on healthcare providers and health systems that deploy AI in clinical settings;
>
> • FDA oversight of AI-enabled clinical decision support tools, including the evolving regulatory framework for software as a medical device (SaMD) and the FDA\'s guidance on predetermined change control plans for adaptive AI/ML-enabled devices;
>
> • Requirements of The Joint Commission, CMS Conditions of Participation, and other healthcare accreditation and certification standards that may apply to the use of AI tools in clinical care settings; and
>
> • State medical practice acts and related professional liability considerations that may affect the allocation of responsibility for AI-assisted clinical decision-making.

Cognitive Foundry is prepared to discuss tailored provisions regarding audit scope, data governance, regulatory cooperation, incident notification, and other regulatory compliance matters on a case-by-case basis for strategic accounts such as Meridian. Cognitive Foundry\'s objective is to establish a partnership framework that supports Meridian\'s clinical mission, safeguards patient safety, and enables both parties to meet their respective legal and regulatory obligations.

This document does not constitute an offer, a binding term sheet, or a waiver of any of Cognitive Foundry\'s standard positions. All terms described herein remain subject to negotiation and the execution of a mutually satisfactory definitive agreement. This summary may not be relied upon by Meridian or any third party as evidence of a binding commitment by Cognitive Foundry. Cognitive Foundry reserves the right to modify, withdraw, or supplement any of the positions described herein at any time prior to the execution of a definitive agreement.

**Prepared by:**

Sandra Hu Vice President, Commercial Legal Cognitive Foundry, Inc. 2750 Sand Hill Road, Building 3 Menlo Park, CA 94025 shu@cognitivefoundry.com (650) 555-0187

CONFIDENTIAL --- This document contains proprietary and confidential information of Cognitive Foundry, Inc. It is provided solely for the use of Meridian Health Systems, Inc. in connection with the parties\' ongoing licensing discussions. Unauthorized reproduction, distribution, or disclosure of this document or its contents is strictly prohibited. © 2025 Cognitive Foundry, Inc. All rights reserved. ClinIQ™ is a trademark of Cognitive Foundry, Inc.

**[MASTER AI SERVICES AGREEMENT]{.underline}**

**between**

**COGNITIVE FOUNDRY, INC.**

**and**

**MERIDIAN HEALTH SYSTEMS, INC.**

**Dated as of August 15, 2025**

CONFIDENTIAL TREATMENT REQUESTED
