**[RECITALS]{.underline}**

**WHEREAS**, Cognitive Foundry, Inc., a Delaware corporation with principal offices at 2750 Sand Hill Road, Building 3, Menlo Park, CA 94025 (\"**Vendor**\"), is in the business of developing and commercializing artificial-intelligence-based clinical decision-support solutions, including its proprietary ClinIQ™ platform, and has significant expertise in applying machine learning and advanced analytics to clinical data for the purpose of improving patient outcomes and operational efficiency in acute-care settings;

**WHEREAS**, Meridian Health Systems, Inc., a Delaware corporation with principal offices at 900 Brandywine Boulevard, Suite 400, Wilmington, DE 19801 (\"**Customer**\"), operates a network of fourteen (14) acute-care hospitals across the States of Delaware, Pennsylvania, and New Jersey (collectively, the \"**Covered Facilities**\"), and desires to license and deploy Vendor\'s ClinIQ™ platform (version 4.2) to support clinical decision-making across its network, including but not limited to sepsis prediction, radiology image analysis, and medication interaction checking;

**WHEREAS**, Customer\'s annual patient encounter volume is approximately 2.1 million across the Covered Facilities, and Customer intends to integrate the Platform with its existing Epic Systems Corporation electronic health record instance known as \"MeridianConnect,\" its Philips PACS radiology image archiving and communication system, and its BD Pyxis medication management systems, each as further described in the Statement of Work attached hereto as Exhibit C;

**WHEREAS**, Vendor and Customer have entered into a Business Associate Agreement dated June 2, 2025 (attached hereto as Exhibit F), governing the treatment of Protected Health Information under the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996, as amended, and its implementing regulations (collectively, \"**HIPAA**\"), the Health Information Technology for Economic and Clinical Health Act (\"**HITECH**\"), and applicable state laws;

**WHEREAS**, the Parties desire to set forth the terms and conditions under which Vendor will license, implement, host, and support the Platform for Customer\'s use at the Covered Facilities, including the allocation of intellectual property rights, data governance responsibilities, service level commitments, and each Party\'s respective obligations regarding regulatory compliance;

**NOW, THEREFORE**, in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements hereinafter set forth, and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the Parties agree as follows:

**[ARTICLE 1 --- DEFINITIONS]{.underline}**

**1.1** **\"Affiliate\"** means any entity Controlling, Controlled by, or under common Control with a Party, where \"**Control**\" means the direct or indirect ownership of more than fifty percent (50%) of the voting securities or equivalent ownership interests of an entity, or the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of an entity, whether through ownership of voting securities, by contract, or otherwise.

**1.2** **\"Aggregated Data\"** means data derived from Customer Data that has been de-identified in accordance with 45 C.F.R. § 164.514(a)--(c), using either the safe harbor method or the expert determination method, and aggregated with data from other Vendor customers or other sources such that such data does not identify Customer, any individual patient, or any Covered Facility. For the avoidance of doubt, Aggregated Data shall not contain any direct or indirect patient identifiers, facility-specific identifiers, or information that could reasonably be used, alone or in combination with other information, to re-identify Customer, any Covered Facility, or any individual patient.

**1.3** **\"Annual Recurring Fee\"** means the annual subscription and support fee of Eight Million Five Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars (\$8,550,000) per Contract Year, as set forth in Section 7.2 and Exhibit B, payable in quarterly installments as further described therein.

**1.4** **\"Authorized Users\"** means Customer\'s employees, independent contractors, and credentialed clinical staff (including physicians, nurses, pharmacists, and allied health professionals) who are authorized by Customer to access and use the Platform in connection with Customer\'s clinical operations at the Covered Facilities. The total number of Authorized Users shall not exceed six thousand two hundred (6,200) Named Licensed Users at any time during the Term, as identified in Exhibit B.

**1.5** **\"BAA\"** means the Business Associate Agreement between the Parties dated June 2, 2025, attached hereto as Exhibit F and incorporated herein by reference, as such agreement may be amended from time to time by mutual written agreement of the Parties.

**1.6** **\"ClinIQ™ Platform\"** or **\"Platform\"** means Vendor\'s proprietary ClinIQ™ clinical decision-support software, version 4.2, including all AI/ML models, algorithms, application programming interfaces (\"**APIs**\"), user interfaces, dashboards, documentation, and Updates provided by Vendor to Customer hereunder, as further described in the Exhibits.

**1.7** **\"Confidential Information\"** means all non-public information disclosed by one Party (the \"**Disclosing Party**\") to the other Party (the \"**Receiving Party**\") in connection with this Agreement, whether disclosed orally, in writing, electronically, or by other means, and whether or not marked or designated as confidential. Confidential Information includes, without limitation, technical, business, financial, and operational information, and expressly includes Model IP, training data, source code, algorithms, product roadmaps, Customer Data, the financial terms of this Agreement, and any information that a reasonable person would understand to be confidential given the nature of the information and the circumstances of disclosure.

**1.8** **\"Contract Year\"** means each successive twelve (12)-month period commencing on the Go-Live Date or the anniversary thereof. By way of example, Contract Year 1 shall commence on the Go-Live Date and end on the day immediately preceding the first anniversary of the Go-Live Date.

**1.9** **\"Covered Facilities\"** means the fourteen (14) acute-care hospitals operated by Customer in the States of Delaware, Pennsylvania, and New Jersey, as listed in Exhibit A attached hereto. References to \"Covered Facilities\" shall include any successor facilities that replace a listed facility, subject to the provisions of Section 2.3.

**1.10** **\"Customer Data\"** means all data, including Protected Health Information, that is input to, generated by, processed through, or stored within the Platform by or on behalf of Customer, its Authorized Users, or its patients, including but not limited to clinical data, demographic data, laboratory results, diagnostic imaging data, medication records, and administrative data.

**1.11** **\"Derivative Data\"** means any data, model weights, parameters, embeddings, statistical artifacts, or other outputs derived from, generated by, or resulting from the processing of Customer Data through Vendor\'s AI/ML models, including without limitation fine-tuned model adaptations, transfer learning artifacts, and feature representations. Derivative Data is distinct from Outputs and shall be treated as a component of Model IP.

**1.12** **\"Documentation\"** means the user manuals, technical guides, API documentation, release notes, and other written materials provided by Vendor to Customer in connection with the Platform, as updated from time to time.

**1.13** **\"Effective Date\"** means August 15, 2025, the date first written above.

**1.14** **\"Fees\"** means, collectively, the Implementation Fee, the Annual Recurring Fee, and any other fees payable by Customer under this Agreement or the Exhibits.

**1.15** **\"Go-Live Date\"** means the date on which the Platform is placed into production use across all Covered Facilities following successful completion of user acceptance testing, targeted for January 6, 2026, subject to the implementation timeline and milestones set forth in Exhibit C.

**1.16** **\"Hosting Environment\"** means the cloud computing infrastructure provided by Amazon Web Services, Inc. (\"**AWS**\"), specifically the AWS GovCloud region us-east-1, in which the Platform is hosted, as further described in Section 4.1 and Exhibit E.

**1.17** **\"Implementation Fee\"** means the one-time fee of Four Million Two Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$4,200,000) for the implementation services described in Exhibit C, payable in accordance with the milestone schedule set forth in Exhibit B.

**1.18** **\"Model IP\"** means all model architectures, neural network weights, parameters, embeddings, hyperparameters, training methodologies, training data, validation datasets, test datasets, fine-tuned adaptations, transfer learning artifacts, and Derivative Data associated with the Platform, whether existing as of the Effective Date or developed during the Term.

**1.19** **\"Outputs\"** means the clinical decision-support alerts, recommendations, risk scores, probability assessments, summaries, visualizations, reports, and other results generated by the Platform from Customer Data inputs and presented to Authorized Users through the Platform\'s user interface or via integration with Customer\'s systems.

**1.20** **\"Party\"** means either Vendor or Customer, as the context requires, and **\"Parties\"** means Vendor and Customer collectively.

**1.21** **\"Protected Health Information\"** or **\"PHI\"** has the meaning ascribed to such term in 45 C.F.R. § 160.103, as modified by the HITECH Act, and as it relates to Customer Data processed under this Agreement and the BAA.

**1.22** **\"Scheduled Maintenance Window\"** means the pre-designated time periods during which Vendor may perform scheduled maintenance on the Platform, as set forth in Exhibit D (Sundays 2:00 AM -- 6:00 AM Eastern Time).

**1.23** **\"Security Incident\"** has the meaning ascribed to such term in the BAA.

**1.24** **\"SLA\"** means the Service Level Agreement set forth in Exhibit D, as incorporated herein by reference.

**1.25** **\"Term\"** means the Initial Term together with any Renewal Terms, as defined in Article 8.

**1.26** **\"Total Contract Value\"** or **\"TCV\"** means Thirty-Eight Million Four Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$38,400,000), representing the aggregate Fees payable by Customer over the four (4)-year Initial Term, as detailed in Section 7.3 and Exhibit B.

**1.27** **\"Transparency Report\"** has the meaning ascribed to such term in Section 10.1.

**1.28** **\"Updates\"** means patches, bug fixes, minor version releases, security updates, and routine maintenance updates to the Platform provided by Vendor during the Term as part of the Annual Recurring Fee. Updates expressly exclude new major-version releases (e.g., ClinIQ™ version 5.0), which shall be made available to Customer only upon separate mutual written agreement and, if applicable, payment of additional fees.

**1.29** **Additional Definitions.** Other capitalized terms used herein shall have the meanings ascribed to them where first defined in the body of this Agreement or in the Exhibits attached hereto. In the event of a conflict between a definition in the body of this Agreement and a definition in an Exhibit, the definition in the body of this Agreement shall control unless the Exhibit expressly states otherwise.

**[ARTICLE 2 --- LICENSE GRANT AND SCOPE]{.underline}**

**2.1** **License Grant.** Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, including Customer\'s payment of all applicable Fees, Vendor hereby grants to Customer a non-exclusive, non-transferable (except as expressly provided in Section 16.4), non-sublicensable, limited right and license during the Term to access and use the Platform solely for Customer\'s internal clinical decision-support purposes at the Covered Facilities. The license granted herein includes the right for Authorized Users to access the Platform via web-based interfaces and APIs as specified in the Documentation, to input Customer Data into the Platform, and to receive and use Outputs generated by the Platform in connection with clinical care, quality improvement, and operational decision-making at the Covered Facilities. The foregoing license does not include any right to access, view, copy, or reverse-engineer any Model IP, source code, or underlying algorithms of the Platform.

**2.2** **Named Licensed Users.** The license granted in Section 2.1 is limited to a maximum of six thousand two hundred (6,200) Named Licensed Users at any given time during the Term. Customer shall assign unique user credentials to each Authorized User; the sharing of user credentials among multiple individuals is strictly prohibited. Customer shall maintain an accurate and current registry of all Named Licensed Users and shall make such registry available to Vendor upon reasonable request. In the event Customer desires to add Authorized Users beyond the 6,200-user threshold, such addition shall require the mutual written agreement of the Parties and payment by Customer of incremental per-user fees as specified in Exhibit B. Customer may replace Named Licensed Users (e.g., upon personnel changes) without incurring additional fees, provided the total number of active Named Licensed Users does not at any time exceed the authorized threshold.

**2.3** **Covered Facilities.** Use of the Platform is restricted to the fourteen (14) Covered Facilities listed in Exhibit A. Extension of the Platform to additional facilities operated by Customer or its Affiliates, including any facilities acquired by Customer after the Effective Date, shall require Vendor\'s prior written consent, which shall not be unreasonably withheld, and the execution of a written amendment to this Agreement specifying the additional facilities, any incremental Fees, and any required implementation services. In the event a Covered Facility is closed, consolidated, or replaced by Customer during the Term, Customer may, upon written notice to Vendor, designate a replacement facility without additional Fees, provided the total number of Covered Facilities does not exceed fourteen (14) at any time.

**2.4** **Output License.** Customer is hereby granted a non-exclusive, perpetual (surviving termination or expiration of this Agreement), royalty-free license to use, store, reproduce, display, and distribute Outputs generated by the Platform solely for Customer\'s internal clinical, operational, regulatory, legal, compliance, and quality-improvement purposes. Customer may include Outputs in patient medical records, clinical documentation, quality reports, and regulatory filings as required in the ordinary course of its clinical operations. For the avoidance of doubt, the Output License does not convey any rights in or to Model IP, and Customer shall not use Outputs to reverse-engineer, reconstruct, or derive any Model IP or any component thereof.

**2.5** **Restrictions.** Customer shall not, and shall not permit any third party to:

> \(a\) reverse-engineer, decompile, disassemble, or otherwise attempt to derive the source code, algorithms, or underlying structure of the Platform or any component thereof;
>
> \(b\) attempt to access, extract, copy, reconstruct, or derive any Model IP, including but not limited to model weights, parameters, embeddings, training data, or validation datasets;
>
> \(c\) sublicense, sell, resell, lease, lend, distribute, or otherwise make the Platform or any portion thereof available to any third party, whether on a service bureau, time-sharing, or other basis;
>
> \(d\) use the Platform for purposes other than internal clinical decision support at the Covered Facilities, including but not limited to using the Platform for commercial resale, third-party consulting, or competitive benchmarking;
>
> \(e\) modify, adapt, translate, or create derivative works of the Platform or any component thereof, except as expressly authorized in writing by Vendor;
>
> \(f\) remove, alter, obscure, or deface any proprietary notices, labels, trademarks, or branding affixed to or displayed by the Platform;
>
> \(g\) use the Platform in any manner that violates applicable law, including HIPAA, HITECH, and applicable state laws;
>
> \(h\) introduce any virus, malware, or harmful code into the Platform or the Hosting Environment; or
>
> \(i\) use the Platform to develop or train any AI or machine learning model that competes with the Platform or any other Vendor product.

**2.6** **Reservation of Rights.** All rights in and to the Platform not expressly granted to Customer under this Agreement are reserved to Vendor. No implied licenses are granted by virtue of this Agreement, whether by estoppel, exhaustion, or otherwise. Vendor reserves the right to provide the Platform or similar services to other customers, including other healthcare providers and health systems that may compete with Customer, without restriction.

**[ARTICLE 3 --- IMPLEMENTATION AND GO-LIVE]{.underline}**

**3.1** **Implementation Services.** Vendor shall provide the implementation services described in the Statement of Work attached as Exhibit C (the \"**SOW**\"), which shall include, without limitation, the following activities: (a) provisioning and configuration of the Hosting Environment; (b) data migration and ingestion pipeline development; (c) AI/ML model configuration and Customer-specific fine-tuning; (d) development, testing, and deployment of integration interfaces with Customer\'s clinical systems; (e) validation testing, including clinical validation of model outputs against agreed-upon benchmarks; (f) user acceptance testing support; and (g) initial training and knowledge transfer. Vendor shall perform the implementation services in a professional and workmanlike manner, consistent with industry standards for healthcare information technology implementations of comparable scope and complexity.

**3.2** **Integration Points.** Vendor shall integrate the Platform with the following Customer systems, each as further specified in Exhibit C:

> \(a\) **Epic EHR \"MeridianConnect.\"** Vendor shall develop and deploy bidirectional interfaces between the Platform and Customer\'s Epic Systems Corporation electronic health record instance, known as \"MeridianConnect,\" using HL7 FHIR R4 standards for structured clinical data exchange and HL7 v2 messaging for legacy data feeds, including ADT (admit/discharge/transfer), ORM (order messages), ORU (observation results), and MDM (medical document management) message types. The integration shall enable the Platform to receive real-time clinical data streams from MeridianConnect and to deliver Outputs, including clinical alerts and recommendations, directly into Epic\'s clinical workflows via CDS Hooks or comparable mechanisms.
>
> \(b\) **Philips PACS.** Vendor shall integrate the Platform with Customer\'s Philips PACS for radiology image analysis workflows, including the ingestion of DICOM-formatted images and the delivery of AI-generated radiology findings and annotations back to the PACS for radiologist review.
>
> \(c\) **BD Pyxis.** Vendor shall integrate the Platform with Customer\'s BD Pyxis medication management systems to support medication interaction checking and alert generation, including bidirectional data exchange for medication administration records and real-time allergy and drug interaction alerts.

**3.3** **Target Go-Live Date.** The Parties shall use commercially reasonable efforts to achieve the Go-Live Date of January 6, 2026, in accordance with the project timeline and milestones set forth in Exhibit C. If the Go-Live Date is delayed for reasons primarily attributable to Vendor\'s failure to perform its obligations under the SOW, Customer\'s obligation to pay the Annual Recurring Fee shall not commence until the actual Go-Live Date, and Vendor shall bear its own costs associated with the delay. If the Go-Live Date is delayed for reasons primarily attributable to Customer\'s failure to perform its obligations under the SOW, including failure to provide timely access to systems, data, facilities, or personnel, such delay shall not relieve Customer of its obligation to pay the Annual Recurring Fee commencing on the originally targeted Go-Live Date or the actual Go-Live Date, whichever is earlier, and Customer shall reimburse Vendor for reasonable incremental costs incurred by Vendor as a result of such delay, subject to Vendor\'s obligation to provide Customer with reasonable notice of and documentation supporting such incremental costs.

**3.4** **Acceptance Testing.** Prior to the Go-Live Date, Customer shall conduct user acceptance testing (\"**UAT**\") in accordance with the acceptance criteria and testing procedures set forth in Exhibit C. Customer shall notify Vendor in writing of its acceptance or rejection of the Platform within thirty (30) calendar days following Vendor\'s written notification that the Platform is ready for UAT (the \"**UAT Delivery Date**\"). In the event Customer rejects the Platform, Customer\'s rejection notice shall specify with reasonable detail the deficiencies or failures to meet the acceptance criteria. Vendor shall use commercially reasonable efforts to correct identified deficiencies within thirty (30) days of receipt of Customer\'s rejection notice, after which a second round of UAT shall be conducted. If the Platform fails to meet the acceptance criteria after two (2) rounds of UAT, either Party may escalate the matter to the executive sponsors designated in the SOW for resolution.

**3.5** **Implementation Personnel.** Vendor shall designate a qualified project manager who shall serve as the primary point of contact for all implementation-related matters and shall maintain a qualified implementation team with sufficient expertise in healthcare IT, clinical systems integration, and AI/ML deployment. Customer shall designate a project lead and shall provide Vendor with reasonable access to Customer\'s systems, facilities, data, and key personnel (including clinical, IT, and administrative staff) as reasonably necessary for Vendor to perform the implementation services. Each Party shall promptly notify the other Party in writing of any changes to its designated project manager or project lead.

**3.6** **Training.** Vendor shall provide initial training to Customer personnel as described in Exhibit C, including: (a) on-site instructor-led training sessions at designated Covered Facilities for clinical staff, IT support staff, and administrative users; (b) virtual training modules accessible through Vendor\'s learning management system; and (c) train-the-trainer sessions for Customer\'s designated super-users. Training materials shall be provided in English and shall include user guides, quick-reference cards, and video tutorials. Vendor shall provide a minimum of one hundred sixty (160) hours of instructor-led training across the Covered Facilities during the implementation period.

**[ARTICLE 4 --- HOSTING AND INFRASTRUCTURE]{.underline}**

**4.1** **Hosting Environment.** The Platform shall be hosted in the Amazon Web Services, Inc. GovCloud region us-east-1 (the \"**Hosting Environment**\"). Vendor shall be solely responsible for provisioning, managing, configuring, scaling, and maintaining the Hosting Environment, including all infrastructure components, operating systems, middleware, application servers, databases, and network configurations. Vendor shall ensure that the Hosting Environment is configured in accordance with the security and technical requirements set forth in Exhibit E and is sufficient to support Customer\'s anticipated workload, including peak patient encounter volumes across the Covered Facilities.

**4.2** **Data Residency.** All Customer Data, including PHI, shall reside within the continental United States at all times. Customer Data shall not be transferred, stored, processed, or accessed outside the United States without Customer\'s prior written consent. Vendor shall ensure that all backups, disaster recovery replicas, and archival copies of Customer Data are maintained within the continental United States. In the event AWS introduces a requirement or practice that would result in Customer Data being stored or processed outside the United States, Vendor shall promptly notify Customer and take all necessary steps to prevent such transfer.

**4.3** **Encryption.** Vendor shall encrypt all Customer Data at rest using Advanced Encryption Standard with a key length of 256 bits (AES-256) or a cryptographic standard of equivalent or greater strength as recognized by the National Institute of Standards and Technology (\"**NIST**\"). All Customer Data in transit between Customer\'s systems and the Hosting Environment, and between components of the Hosting Environment, shall be encrypted using Transport Layer Security version 1.2 or higher (TLS 1.2+). Customer shall have the right to manage its own encryption keys through a customer-managed key escrow arrangement utilizing AWS Key Management Service (\"**KMS**\"), with Customer-controlled customer master keys (\"**CMKs**\"), the details and procedures for which are set forth in Exhibit E. Vendor shall not access or use Customer\'s CMKs except as necessary to operate the Platform on Customer\'s behalf.

**4.4** **Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery.** Vendor shall maintain business continuity and disaster recovery capabilities sufficient to protect the availability and integrity of the Platform and Customer Data, as set forth in Exhibit D. At a minimum, Vendor shall maintain: (a) a recovery point objective (\"**RPO**\") of one (1) hour, meaning that in the event of a disaster, data loss shall not exceed one (1) hour of transactions; and (b) a recovery time objective (\"**RTO**\") of four (4) hours, meaning that the Platform shall be restored to operational status within four (4) hours of a declared disaster. Vendor shall test its disaster recovery procedures at least annually and shall provide Customer with a summary of test results upon reasonable request.

**4.5** **Subprocessors.** Customer acknowledges and consents to Vendor\'s use of Amazon Web Services, Inc. as a subprocessor for the hosting and operation of the Platform. Vendor shall not engage any additional subprocessors or sub-contractors that will access, process, store, or transmit Customer Data without providing Customer with at least thirty (30) days\' prior written notice, which notice shall identify the proposed subprocessor, the services to be performed, and the data to be accessed. Customer shall have the right to object to any proposed subprocessor on reasonable grounds related to data security, privacy, or regulatory compliance, and the Parties shall work in good faith to resolve any objection. If the Parties are unable to resolve Customer\'s objection within thirty (30) days, Customer may terminate the affected services (but not the entire Agreement) without penalty. Vendor shall ensure that all subprocessors are bound by written agreements containing data protection and confidentiality obligations at least as protective as those set forth in this Agreement and the BAA.

**[ARTICLE 5 --- DATA GOVERNANCE AND HIPAA COMPLIANCE]{.underline}**

**5.1** **Customer Data Ownership.** As between the Parties, Customer retains all right, title, and interest in and to Customer Data, including all PHI contained therein. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as transferring ownership of Customer Data to Vendor. Vendor\'s access to and processing of Customer Data shall be strictly limited to the purposes of performing its obligations under this Agreement, as authorized in the BAA, and as otherwise expressly permitted herein (including, without limitation, the creation of Aggregated Data and Derivative Data in accordance with the terms of this Agreement). Vendor shall not sell, rent, lease, or otherwise commercially exploit Customer Data, except as expressly authorized in this Agreement with respect to Aggregated Data.

**5.2** **HIPAA Compliance.** The Parties\' respective obligations regarding the use, disclosure, and protection of PHI are governed by the BAA attached as Exhibit F, executed as of June 2, 2025. Each Party shall comply with all applicable requirements of HIPAA, HITECH, and their implementing regulations, including the Privacy Rule (45 C.F.R. Part 164, Subpart E), the Security Rule (45 C.F.R. Part 164, Subpart C), and the Breach Notification Rule (45 C.F.R. Part 164, Subpart D). In the event of a conflict between this Agreement and the BAA with respect to the treatment of PHI, the BAA shall control. Vendor shall ensure that its employees, agents, and subcontractors who have access to PHI are trained on HIPAA requirements and are bound by appropriate confidentiality obligations.

**5.3** **De-Identification Standards.** Any de-identification of Customer Data by Vendor, whether for the creation of Aggregated Data or for any other purpose, shall comply with either: (a) the safe harbor method under 45 C.F.R. § 164.514(b), requiring the removal or generalization of the eighteen (18) specified categories of identifiers; or (b) the expert determination method under 45 C.F.R. § 164.514(a), requiring a qualified statistical or scientific expert to determine that the risk of re-identification is very small. Vendor shall, upon Customer\'s reasonable request, provide Customer with documentation sufficient to demonstrate that de-identification has been performed in compliance with the applicable method. Vendor shall not attempt to re-identify any de-identified data derived from Customer Data.

**5.4** **Aggregated Data Use.** Subject to Vendor\'s compliance with the de-identification standards set forth in Section 5.3, Vendor shall have an unrestricted, perpetual, irrevocable, worldwide, royalty-free right and license to use, modify, distribute, sublicense, and create derivative works from Aggregated Data for any purpose, including but not limited to: (a) research and development; (b) product improvement and enhancement; (c) AI/ML model training, validation, and benchmarking; (d) creation of industry benchmarks and reports; (e) publication of research findings; and (f) commercial exploitation and licensing to third parties, in each case provided that such Aggregated Data does not identify Customer, any Covered Facility, or any individual patient, and that Vendor\'s use of Aggregated Data does not violate applicable law. Customer acknowledges and agrees that Vendor\'s ability to continuously improve the Platform, develop new AI/ML models, and create innovative clinical decision-support products depends upon Vendor\'s access to and use of Aggregated Data derived from its customer base, and that the foregoing grant is a material inducement for Vendor to enter into this Agreement at the Fees stated herein. For the avoidance of doubt, Vendor\'s rights in Aggregated Data shall survive termination or expiration of this Agreement in perpetuity, as further set forth in Section 14.7.

**5.5** **Data Security.** Vendor shall implement and maintain a comprehensive information security program that includes administrative, technical, and physical safeguards designed to protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of Customer Data in accordance with HIPAA, HITECH, applicable state laws (including the laws of Delaware, Pennsylvania, and New Jersey), and industry best practices. Vendor\'s security program shall be aligned with the NIST Cybersecurity Framework (CSF) and shall address, at a minimum, the security requirements set forth in Exhibit E. Vendor shall conduct annual risk assessments in accordance with 45 C.F.R. § 164.308(a)(1)(ii)(A) and shall remediate identified risks in a timely manner.

**5.6** **Breach Notification.** Vendor shall notify Customer of any Security Incident or Breach of Unsecured PHI (each as defined in the BAA) in accordance with the timeframes and procedures set forth in the BAA. Without limiting the foregoing, Vendor shall notify Customer of any confirmed Breach of Unsecured PHI without unreasonable delay and in no event later than the timeframe specified in the BAA. Vendor shall cooperate fully with Customer in investigating and mitigating any Security Incident or Breach, including providing Customer with all information reasonably necessary for Customer to comply with its notification obligations under HIPAA, HITECH, and applicable state breach notification laws.

**5.7** **State Law Compliance.** Vendor acknowledges that Customer operates Covered Facilities in the States of Delaware, Pennsylvania, and New Jersey, and that Customer is subject to the data privacy, security, and healthcare regulatory requirements of each such state. Vendor shall comply with all applicable state data privacy and security laws in connection with its processing of Customer Data and performance of its obligations under this Agreement. The Parties specifically acknowledge the New Jersey AI in Healthcare Transparency Act (effective March 1, 2025) (the \"**NJ AI Act**\") and its requirements regarding the disclosure of information about AI-based clinical decision-support tools used in patient care at healthcare facilities in the State of New Jersey. The Parties shall cooperate in good faith to ensure compliance with the NJ AI Act, including with respect to the transparency and disclosure obligations applicable to Customer\'s New Jersey Covered Facilities, as further addressed in Section 10.3.

**[ARTICLE 6 --- INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY OWNERSHIP]{.underline}**

>
> \(d\) all Derivative Data, including statistical artifacts, feature representations, and intermediate computational outputs;
>

Customer hereby irrevocably assigns, and agrees to assign, to Vendor all right, title, and interest that Customer may have or may hereafter acquire in such fine-tuned adaptations and Derivative Data, including all intellectual property rights therein. Customer shall execute any documents and take any actions reasonably requested by Vendor to effectuate and confirm such assignment. Customer acknowledges that Vendor may use such fine-tuned models and Derivative Data to improve the Platform for the benefit of all Vendor customers, subject to Vendor\'s compliance with the de-identification and aggregation requirements of this Agreement.

**6.3** **Output License.** Customer\'s rights in Outputs generated by the Platform are as set forth in Section 2.4. Outputs constitute the results of processing Customer Data through the Platform and do not constitute Model IP. Customer\'s use of Outputs shall not grant Customer any rights in or to the underlying models, algorithms, or methodologies used to generate such Outputs.

**6.4** **Feedback.** To the extent Customer or any Authorized User provides Vendor with feedback, suggestions, enhancement requests, recommendations, bug reports, or other input regarding the Platform or its functionality (\"**Feedback**\"), Customer hereby grants to Vendor a perpetual, irrevocable, royalty-free, fully paid-up, worldwide, non-exclusive, transferable, sublicensable license to use, reproduce, modify, incorporate, distribute, and commercialize such Feedback in any manner and for any purpose, without attribution, compensation, or restriction. Customer represents and warrants that it has the authority to grant the foregoing license with respect to any Feedback provided by Customer or its Authorized Users.

**6.5** **Customer Pre-Existing IP.** Vendor acknowledges Customer\'s ownership of its pre-existing intellectual property, including without limitation its clinical protocols, clinical pathways, order sets, formularies, operational data, business processes, and MeridianConnect configurations and customizations (collectively, \"**Customer Pre-Existing IP**\"). Nothing in this Agreement grants Vendor any rights in Customer Pre-Existing IP except the limited right to access and use Customer Pre-Existing IP as reasonably necessary for Vendor to perform its obligations under this Agreement, including implementation, integration, model configuration, and ongoing support. Vendor shall not use Customer Pre-Existing IP for any purpose other than the performance of its obligations hereunder without Customer\'s prior written consent.

**[ARTICLE 7 --- FEES AND PAYMENT]{.underline}**

**7.1** **Implementation Fee.** Customer shall pay Vendor a one-time Implementation Fee of Four Million Two Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$4,200,000) for the implementation services described in Exhibit C. The Implementation Fee shall be payable in installments in accordance with the milestone-based payment schedule set forth in Exhibit B, with each installment becoming due and payable upon Vendor\'s achievement of the corresponding milestone as specified therein. The Implementation Fee is non-refundable upon payment, except as expressly provided in Section 14.4 or as otherwise agreed by the Parties.

**7.2** **Annual Recurring Fee.** Commencing on the Go-Live Date (or such other date as may be determined in accordance with Section 3.3), Customer shall pay Vendor an Annual Recurring Fee of Eight Million Five Hundred Fifty Thousand Dollars (\$8,550,000) per Contract Year. The Annual Recurring Fee shall be payable in equal quarterly installments of Two Million One Hundred Thirty-Seven Thousand Five Hundred Dollars (\$2,137,500) each, payable in advance within thirty (30) calendar days of the first day of each calendar quarter during the Contract Year. The Annual Recurring Fee includes the Platform license, hosting, standard support (including Severity 1--4 incident response), Updates, and the AI model performance reporting described in Section 9.3.

**7.3** **Total Contract Value.** The Total Contract Value over the four (4)-year Initial Term is Thirty-Eight Million Four Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$38,400,000), consisting of: (a) the Implementation Fee of Four Million Two Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$4,200,000); plus (b) four (4) Contract Years of Annual Recurring Fees totaling Thirty-Four Million Two Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$34,200,000) (i.e., \$8,550,000 × 4). The Total Contract Value does not include fees for Renewal Terms, incremental per-user fees, transition assistance fees, or other amounts that may become payable under this Agreement.

**7.4** **Renewal Term Pricing.** Annual Recurring Fees for each Renewal Term shall be subject to an annual increase, effective as of the first day of each Contract Year within a Renewal Term, not to exceed the greater of: (a) three percent (3%); or (b) the percentage increase in the Consumer Price Index --- All Urban Consumers (CPI-U), U.S. City Average, All Items, Not Seasonally Adjusted, as published by the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics, for the twelve (12)-month period ending on the last day of the calendar quarter immediately preceding the applicable Contract Year anniversary. In no event shall the Annual Recurring Fee decrease during a Renewal Term.

**7.5** **Taxes.** All Fees stated in this Agreement are exclusive of all applicable federal, state, and local sales, use, excise, value-added, goods and services, and similar taxes, duties, and governmental charges (collectively, \"**Taxes**\"). Customer shall be responsible for the payment of all Taxes arising out of or related to this Agreement, excluding taxes imposed on Vendor\'s net income, franchise taxes, and taxes based on Vendor\'s employment of its own personnel. Vendor shall separately itemize applicable Taxes on each invoice. If Customer is exempt from any Tax, Customer shall provide Vendor with a valid exemption certificate or other documentation reasonably satisfactory to Vendor.

**7.6** **Late Payment.** Any amount not paid when due shall bear interest from the due date until paid at the lesser of: (a) one and one-half percent (1.5%) per month (18% per annum); or (b) the maximum rate permitted by applicable law. The foregoing interest shall be in addition to, and not in lieu of, any other remedies available to Vendor. Vendor shall have the right, upon thirty (30) days\' prior written notice to Customer, to suspend Customer\'s access to the Platform in the event any undisputed amount remains unpaid for more than sixty (60) days past its due date, provided that such suspension shall not relieve Customer of its obligation to pay all outstanding Fees and accrued interest.

**7.7** **Disputed Invoices.** Customer may withhold payment of any portion of an invoice that is the subject of a good-faith dispute, provided that: (a) Customer delivers written notice of the dispute to Vendor with reasonable detail describing the basis for the dispute within fifteen (15) calendar days of Customer\'s receipt of the invoice; and (b) Customer pays all undisputed amounts by the applicable due date. The Parties shall use good-faith efforts to resolve any invoice dispute within thirty (30) days of Vendor\'s receipt of Customer\'s dispute notice. If the dispute is resolved in Vendor\'s favor, Customer shall pay the disputed amount within fifteen (15) days of resolution, together with interest calculated in accordance with Section 7.6 from the original due date. If the dispute is resolved in Customer\'s favor, Vendor shall issue a credit to Customer\'s account in the amount determined to be owed.

**[ARTICLE 8 --- TERM AND RENEWAL]{.underline}**

**8.1** **Initial Term.** This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date (August 15, 2025) and, unless earlier terminated in accordance with Article 14, shall continue in full force and effect for a period of four (4) years from the Go-Live Date (the \"**Initial Term**\"). The Parties acknowledge that the period between the Effective Date and the Go-Live Date is the implementation period, during which the Parties\' rights and obligations regarding implementation (Article 3) and all other applicable provisions of this Agreement shall be in effect.

**8.2** **Renewal Terms.** Upon expiration of the Initial Term, this Agreement shall automatically renew for up to two (2) successive periods of two (2) years each (each, a \"**Renewal Term**,\" and together with the Initial Term, the \"**Term**\"), unless either Party provides written notice of non-renewal to the other Party at least one hundred eighty (180) calendar days prior to the expiration of the then-current Term. For the avoidance of doubt, the maximum total duration of this Agreement, including the Initial Term and both Renewal Terms, is eight (8) years from the Go-Live Date (plus the implementation period preceding the Go-Live Date). Annual Recurring Fees for Renewal Terms shall be adjusted in accordance with Section 7.4.

**8.3** **Effect of Expiration.** Upon expiration of the Initial Term or any Renewal Term (in the absence of renewal), the following shall apply: (a) all licenses granted to Customer under Article 2 shall immediately terminate, and Customer shall cease all use of the Platform; (b) the Parties\' respective obligations regarding transition assistance (Section 14.5), data return and destruction (Section 14.6), confidentiality (Article 11), indemnification (Article 13), limitation of liability (Article 12), insurance (Article 15), and survival provisions (Section 16.7) shall continue in effect as set forth herein; and (c) all Fees accrued but unpaid as of the expiration date shall remain due and payable.

**[ARTICLE 9 --- SERVICE LEVEL AGREEMENT]{.underline}**

**9.1** **SLA Terms.** Vendor shall provide the Platform in accordance with the Service Level Agreement attached as Exhibit D, which sets forth Vendor\'s commitments regarding the following service levels:

> \(a\) **Uptime.** Platform Availability of not less than ninety-nine and nine-tenths percent (99.9%) per calendar month, measured as set forth in Exhibit D. Platform Availability shall be calculated as: \[(Total Minutes in Calendar Month − Unscheduled Downtime Minutes) ÷ Total Minutes in Calendar Month\] × 100. Scheduled Maintenance Windows, as defined in Section 9.1(c), shall be excluded from the calculation of Platform Availability. Unscheduled Downtime means any period during which the Platform is materially unavailable to Authorized Users, excluding Scheduled Maintenance Windows and periods of unavailability caused by Customer\'s systems, acts or omissions of Customer, force majeure events, or failures of third-party systems not under Vendor\'s control (other than the Hosting Environment).
>
> \(b\) **Response Time.** Vendor shall provide incident response and resolution in accordance with the following severity classifications and target timeframes:

  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Severity Level**      **Description**                                                                                                              **Acknowledgment Target**   **Resolution Target**
  ----------------------- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- --------------------------- -----------------------
  Severity 1 (Critical)   Platform is completely unavailable or a critical clinical function is non-operational, presenting a risk to patient safety   15 minutes                  4 hours

  Severity 2 (High)       Major feature or function is impaired, significantly impacting clinical workflows with no available workaround               1 hour                      8 hours

  Severity 3 (Medium)     Feature or function is impaired but a reasonable workaround is available; limited impact on clinical workflows               4 hours                     2 business days

  Severity 4 (Low)        Minor issue, cosmetic defect, or general inquiry with no material impact on clinical operations                              1 business day              5 business days
  --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

> \(c\) **Scheduled Maintenance.** Vendor shall provide Customer with at least seventy-two (72) hours\' advance written notice of any scheduled maintenance activity. Scheduled maintenance shall be performed during agreed-upon Scheduled Maintenance Windows, which shall be Sundays from 2:00 AM to 6:00 AM Eastern Time, unless otherwise agreed in writing by the Parties. In the event Vendor determines that emergency maintenance is required to address a security vulnerability or critical defect, Vendor shall provide Customer with as much advance notice as is reasonably practicable under the circumstances and shall use commercially reasonable efforts to minimize the duration and impact of such emergency maintenance.

**9.2** **Service Level Credits.** In the event Vendor fails to meet the Platform Availability commitment set forth in Section 9.1(a) during any calendar month, Customer shall be entitled to service level credits as the sole and exclusive remedy for such failure, calculated as follows:

  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Monthly Platform Availability**   **Service Level Credit (% of Monthly Fee)**
  ----------------------------------- ---------------------------------------------
  99.0% to less than 99.9%            5%

  98.0% to less than 99.0%            10%

  Below 98.0%                         15%
  ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

For purposes of this Section 9.2, the \"**Monthly Fee**\" shall mean one-twelfth (1/12) of the Annual Recurring Fee (i.e., Seven Hundred Twelve Thousand Five Hundred Dollars (\$712,500)). Service level credits shall be applied as a credit against the next quarterly invoice following the month in which the SLA failure occurred. Service level credits shall not exceed fifteen percent (15%) of the applicable Monthly Fee in any single calendar month. Service level credits shall not be payable in cash and may not be carried forward beyond the current Contract Year. To receive a service level credit, Customer must submit a written credit request to Vendor within thirty (30) days of the end of the calendar month in which the SLA failure occurred, specifying the dates and duration of the downtime.

**9.3** **AI Model Performance --- Informational Metrics.** Vendor shall use commercially reasonable efforts to maintain AI model performance consistent with the specifications published in the Documentation and as generally described in the SOW. Vendor shall provide Customer with quarterly performance reports (within thirty (30) days of the end of each calendar quarter) that include, where applicable, the following informational metrics for each clinical decision-support module deployed at the Covered Facilities:

> \(a\) **Sepsis Prediction Module:** sensitivity, specificity, positive predictive value, and area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUROC);
>
> \(b\) **Radiology Image Analysis Module:** detection sensitivity, false positive rate, and processing throughput;
>
> \(c\) **Medication Interaction Checking Module:** alert precision, recall, and override rates.

Customer acknowledges and agrees that such metrics are provided for informational purposes only and do not constitute binding performance commitments or warranties. AI model performance is inherently variable and depends on numerous factors, including data quality, patient population characteristics, clinical workflow integration, and model drift over time. No service level credits, termination rights, indemnification obligations, or other remedies shall arise from variations in AI model accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, or other performance metrics. Vendor shall use commercially reasonable efforts to investigate and address material declines in model performance identified in quarterly reports or raised by Customer.

**9.4** **Sole Remedy.** The service level credits set forth in Section 9.2 shall be Customer\'s sole and exclusive remedy, and Vendor\'s sole and exclusive liability, for any failure to meet the service level commitments set forth in Exhibit D and this Article 9.

**[ARTICLE 10 --- AI MODEL TRANSPARENCY AND AUDIT]{.underline}**

**10.1** **Annual Summary Report.** On or before the anniversary of each Go-Live Date during the Term, Vendor shall provide Customer with an annual summary report (the \"**Transparency Report**\") describing in general terms the following:

> \(a\) the types of AI/ML models deployed within the Platform at the Covered Facilities, including a general description of the model types (e.g., deep learning, gradient-boosted decision trees, natural language processing) and the clinical use cases they support;
>
> \(b\) material changes to model architectures, training methodologies, or data preprocessing pipelines implemented during the preceding twelve (12)-month period, including a summary description of the nature and rationale for such changes;
>
> \(c\) summary performance metrics for each clinical decision-support module, consistent with the quarterly reporting described in Section 9.3, aggregated at the annual level;
>
> \(d\) a general description of Vendor\'s internal AI model governance practices, including its model validation, monitoring, and bias detection procedures; and
>
> \(e\) a summary of any material regulatory developments, industry standards, or clinical guidelines that have informed Vendor\'s model development or governance practices during the preceding year.

The Transparency Report shall be marked as Vendor\'s Confidential Information and shall be subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in Article 11.

**10.2** **Scope of Disclosure.** Notwithstanding any other provision of this Agreement, the Transparency Report shall not include, and Customer shall not have any right to access, inspect, audit, review, copy, or receive:

> \(a\) model weights, parameters, biases, or embeddings;
>
> \(b\) training data, validation datasets, test datasets, or data augmentation pipelines;
>
> \(c\) detailed model architecture documentation, network topology diagrams, or hyperparameter specifications beyond the general descriptions included in the Transparency Report;
>
> \(d\) Vendor\'s proprietary algorithms, source code, object code, or trade secrets;
>
> \(e\) Vendor\'s internal model development tools, experimentation platforms, or model registry systems; or
>
> \(f\) any information that would enable Customer or any third party to reconstruct, replicate, or reverse-engineer the Platform\'s AI/ML models or any component thereof.

Customer acknowledges that Model IP constitutes Vendor\'s most valuable trade secrets, that the development of the Platform has required substantial investment of time, resources, and expertise, and that the restrictions set forth in this Section 10.2 are essential to protect Vendor\'s competitive position and continued ability to invest in innovation. Customer agrees that the level of transparency provided through the Transparency Report and the quarterly performance reports under Section 9.3 is reasonable and sufficient for Customer\'s clinical governance and regulatory compliance purposes.

**10.3** **Regulatory Inquiries.** In the event a governmental authority with jurisdiction over Customer or any Covered Facility (including, without limitation, the New Jersey Department of Health, the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services, or any state attorney general) requires disclosure of information regarding the Platform\'s AI models, training data, or model governance practices in connection with the NJ AI Act or any similar federal or state regulatory requirement, the Parties shall cooperate in good faith to respond to such inquiry in a timely and compliant manner, subject to the following:

> \(a\) any disclosure of Vendor\'s Confidential Information, including Model IP, shall be limited to the minimum scope of information required by law or the applicable regulatory authority;
>
> \(b\) Vendor shall have the right, at its own expense, to seek protective orders, confidential treatment designations, or other legal protections to prevent or limit public disclosure of Model IP or other Vendor Confidential Information;
>
> \(c\) Customer shall provide Vendor with prompt written notice (and in any event no less than ten (10) business days\' notice, to the extent permitted by law) of any regulatory inquiry, investigation, or request for information that relates to or may require disclosure of information regarding the Platform\'s AI models;
>
> \(d\) Customer shall not disclose any Vendor Confidential Information in response to a regulatory inquiry without first consulting with Vendor and providing Vendor a reasonable opportunity to participate in the response; and
>
> \(e\) Vendor shall bear the costs associated with preparing its portion of any response to a regulatory inquiry, and Customer shall bear the costs associated with preparing its own portion of such response.

**10.4** **No Independent Audit Right.** Customer shall not have the right to conduct, or to engage any third party (including auditors, consultants, or academic researchers) to conduct, any independent audit, assessment, examination, testing, or review of the Platform\'s AI models, training data, model weights, algorithms, source code, or any other component of Model IP. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Vendor may, at its sole discretion and expense, engage independent third-party assessors or auditors to evaluate the Platform\'s model performance, fairness, bias, and compliance with applicable standards, and Vendor may share summary findings of such assessments with Customer on a confidential basis. Nothing in this Section 10.4 shall limit Customer\'s rights under the BAA with respect to audits of Vendor\'s HIPAA compliance.

**[ARTICLE 11 --- CONFIDENTIALITY]{.underline}**

**11.1** **Obligations.** Each Party (as the Receiving Party) shall: (a) hold the Disclosing Party\'s Confidential Information in strict confidence using at least the same degree of care it uses to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature, but in no event less than a reasonable degree of care; (b) not disclose the Disclosing Party\'s Confidential Information to any third party, except to the Receiving Party\'s employees, Affiliates, contractors, advisors, and agents who have a need to know such information for the purposes of this Agreement and who are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as protective as those set forth herein; (c) not use the Disclosing Party\'s Confidential Information for any purpose other than the performance of the Receiving Party\'s obligations or the exercise of its rights under this Agreement; and (d) promptly notify the Disclosing Party of any actual or suspected unauthorized disclosure or use of the Disclosing Party\'s Confidential Information.

**11.2** **Exceptions.** The obligations set forth in Section 11.1 shall not apply to information that the Receiving Party can demonstrate: (a) was publicly available at the time of disclosure, or subsequently became publicly available through no fault or breach by the Receiving Party; (b) was independently developed by the Receiving Party without reference to or use of the Disclosing Party\'s Confidential Information, as evidenced by the Receiving Party\'s contemporaneous written records; (c) was rightfully received by the Receiving Party from a third party without restriction on disclosure and without breach of any obligation of confidentiality; (d) was already known to the Receiving Party at the time of disclosure, as evidenced by the Receiving Party\'s contemporaneous written records; or (e) is required to be disclosed by applicable law, regulation, court order, or governmental authority, provided that the Receiving Party: (i) provides the Disclosing Party with prompt written notice of such requirement (to the extent legally permitted); (ii) cooperates with the Disclosing Party, at the Disclosing Party\'s expense, in seeking a protective order or other appropriate remedy to limit the scope and manner of disclosure; and (iii) discloses only the minimum amount of Confidential Information required to comply with such requirement.

**11.3** **Injunctive Relief.** Each Party acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure or use of the other Party\'s Confidential Information may cause irreparable harm for which monetary damages would be an inadequate remedy. Accordingly, each Party agrees that the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive or other equitable relief in any court of competent jurisdiction, without the necessity of proving actual damages or posting any bond or other security, in addition to any other remedies available at law or in equity.

**11.4** **Survival.** The confidentiality obligations set forth in this Article 11 shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years from the date of termination or expiration; provided, however, that obligations with respect to information that constitutes a trade secret under applicable law (including, without limitation, the Defend Trade Secrets Act, 18 U.S.C. § 1836 et seq., and applicable state trade secret laws) shall continue for so long as such information remains a trade secret under applicable law, without time limitation.

**12.2** **General Liability Cap.** EXCEPT AS SET FORTH IN SECTION 12.3 AND SECTION 12.4, EACH PARTY\'S TOTAL CUMULATIVE LIABILITY ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT, WHETHER IN CONTRACT, TORT (INCLUDING NEGLIGENCE), STRICT LIABILITY, OR OTHERWISE, SHALL NOT EXCEED AN AMOUNT EQUAL TO ONE TIMES (1×) THE AGGREGATE FEES PAID OR PAYABLE BY CUSTOMER UNDER THIS AGREEMENT DURING THE TWELVE (12)-MONTH PERIOD IMMEDIATELY PRECEDING THE EVENT GIVING RISE TO THE CLAIM (THE \"**GENERAL CAP**\"). FOR PURPOSES OF ILLUSTRATION, AS OF THE FIRST CONTRACT YEAR, THE GENERAL CAP WOULD BE APPROXIMATELY NINE MILLION SIX HUNDRED THOUSAND DOLLARS (\$9,600,000), CALCULATED AS THE SUM OF THE IMPLEMENTATION FEE (TO THE EXTENT PAID DURING SUCH PERIOD) AND THE FIRST-YEAR ANNUAL RECURRING FEE.

**12.3** **Super-Cap Carve-Outs.** The following categories of liability shall not be subject to the General Cap set forth in Section 12.2, but shall instead be subject to a separate aggregate liability cap equal to two times (2×) the aggregate Fees paid or payable by Customer during the trailing twelve (12)-month period (approximately Nineteen Million Two Hundred Thousand Dollars (\$19,200,000) as of the first Contract Year) (the \"**Super-Cap**\"):

> \(a\) a Party\'s indemnification obligations under Article 13;
>
> \(b\) liability arising from a Party\'s gross negligence or willful misconduct; and
>
> \(c\) liability arising from a Party\'s breach of Article 11 (Confidentiality).

**12.4** **Uncapped Liabilities.** Notwithstanding any other provision of this Agreement, neither the General Cap nor the Super-Cap shall apply to:

> \(b\) use of the Platform in combination with any hardware, software, data, products, or services not provided by Vendor or expressly approved by Vendor in writing, where the infringement would not have occurred but for such combination;

**13.3** **Mitigation.** If the Platform becomes, or in Vendor\'s reasonable opinion is likely to become, the subject of an IP Indemnification Claim, Vendor may, at its option and expense:

> \(a\) procure for Customer the right to continue using the Platform in accordance with this Agreement;

> \(b\) modify or replace the Platform or the infringing component thereof to make it non-infringing without materially diminishing the Platform\'s functionality or performance; or
>
> \(c\) if neither option (a) nor option (b) is commercially practicable despite Vendor\'s reasonable efforts, terminate the license granted hereunder with respect to the infringing component and refund to Customer a pro-rata portion of any prepaid Fees attributable to the remainder of the then-current Term, calculated on a straight-line basis from the date of termination through the end of the then-current Term.

The remedies set forth in this Section 13.3 are in addition to, and not in lieu of, Vendor\'s indemnification obligations under Section 13.1.

**13.4** **Customer Indemnification.** Customer shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Vendor, its Affiliates, and their respective officers, directors, employees, agents, and successors and permitted assigns (collectively, \"**Vendor Indemnitees**\") from and against any and all Losses arising out of or related to any third-party claim based upon or resulting from:

>
> \(c\) Customer\'s use of the Platform in violation of this Agreement, including use beyond the scope of the license granted in Article 2 or in breach of the restrictions set forth in Section 2.5.

>

**[ARTICLE 14 --- TERMINATION]{.underline}**

**14.1** **Termination for Cause.** Either Party may terminate this Agreement upon sixty (60) days\' prior written notice to the other Party if the other Party commits a material breach of any provision of this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within such sixty (60)-day notice period. The notice of termination for cause shall specify in reasonable detail the nature of the breach. In the case of a material breach that is not reasonably capable of cure (as determined by the non-breaching Party in its reasonable judgment), the non-breaching Party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon delivery of written notice to the breaching Party specifying the nature of the incurable breach.

**14.2** **Termination for Insolvency.** Either Party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice to the other Party if the other Party:

> \(a\) files a voluntary petition in bankruptcy or is adjudicated bankrupt or insolvent under any applicable bankruptcy, insolvency, or similar law;
>
> \(b\) makes a general assignment for the benefit of creditors;
>
> \(c\) has a receiver, trustee, custodian, or similar official appointed for all or substantially all of its assets, and such appointment is not dismissed or vacated within sixty (60) days;
>
> \(d\) commences or has commenced against it any proceeding under any bankruptcy, reorganization, insolvency, dissolution, or liquidation law, and such proceeding is not dismissed within sixty (60) days; or
>
> \(e\) ceases to conduct business in the ordinary course or is dissolved or wound up (other than in connection with a merger, consolidation, or reorganization in which the surviving entity assumes all obligations under this Agreement).

**14.3** **Termination for Convenience.** This Agreement may be terminated for convenience only upon the mutual written agreement of both Parties, setting forth the effective date of termination and the terms and conditions applicable thereto, including the disposition of Fees, data, and transition obligations. Neither Party shall have the right to unilaterally terminate this Agreement for convenience during the Initial Term or any Renewal Term.

**14.4** **Effect of Termination.** Upon termination or expiration of this Agreement for any reason:

> \(a\) all licenses granted to Customer under Article 2 shall immediately terminate as of the effective date of termination or expiration (or the end of the Transition Period, if applicable);
>
> \(b\) Customer shall promptly cease all use of the Platform and shall delete or destroy all copies of Vendor\'s Confidential Information in Customer\'s possession or control, except as required to be retained by applicable law or regulation or as necessary for Customer\'s use of Outputs in accordance with the surviving Output License;
>
> \(c\) each Party shall return or destroy the other Party\'s Confidential Information in accordance with Section 14.6 and the confidentiality provisions of Article 11;
>
> \(d\) all Fees accrued but unpaid as of the effective date of termination shall remain due and payable. In the event of termination by Customer for Vendor\'s material breach, Vendor shall refund to Customer a pro-rata portion of any prepaid Annual Recurring Fees attributable to the period following the effective date of termination. In the event of termination by Vendor for Customer\'s material breach, Customer shall pay all Fees that would have become due through the end of the then-current Contract Year; and
>
> \(e\) the accrued rights and obligations of the Parties and the provisions identified in Section 16.7 shall survive termination or expiration in accordance with their terms.

**14.5** **Transition Assistance.** Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement for any reason, Vendor shall provide transition assistance services to Customer for a period of up to twelve (12) months following the effective date of termination or expiration (the \"**Transition Period**\"), subject to the following terms:

> \(a\) **Scope.** Transition assistance shall include: (i) reasonable cooperation with Customer and any successor vendor in migrating Customer Data from the Hosting Environment to a successor platform or system designated by Customer, in a commercially standard format reasonably requested by Customer (e.g., HL7 FHIR, CSV, or JSON); (ii) continued operation of the Platform and Hosting Environment during the Transition Period to ensure continuity of clinical decision-support services, subject to Customer\'s continued payment of all applicable Fees during such period; and (iii) knowledge transfer sessions, technical briefings, and documentation as reasonably requested by Customer to facilitate the transition.
>
> \(b\) **Fees.** Customer shall pay all applicable Fees throughout the Transition Period, including the Annual Recurring Fee at then-current rates (adjusted for any applicable Renewal Term pricing escalator, if the Transition Period falls within or after a Renewal Term). In addition, Vendor shall provide professional services in connection with transition assistance at Vendor\'s then-current published professional services rates, which shall be communicated to Customer in writing at the commencement of the Transition Period.
>
> \(c\) **Cooperation.** Vendor shall cooperate in good faith with Customer and any successor vendor during the Transition Period, provided that Vendor shall not be required to disclose any Model IP, source code, or trade secrets to Customer or any successor vendor.

**14.6** **Data Return and Destruction.** Within sixty (60) calendar days following the effective date of termination or expiration of this Agreement (or the end of the Transition Period, if applicable), Vendor shall, at Customer\'s written election:

> \(a\) **Return.** Return to Customer all Customer Data in Vendor\'s possession or control, in a commercially standard, machine-readable format reasonably requested by Customer, via a secure transfer mechanism agreed upon by the Parties; or
>
> \(b\) **Destruction.** Certify to Customer in writing, executed by an authorized officer of Vendor, the complete and irrecoverable destruction of all Customer Data in Vendor\'s possession or control, including all copies, backups, and archival media, in accordance with NIST Special Publication 800-88 (Guidelines for Media Sanitization) or an equivalent standard.

Notwithstanding the foregoing, Vendor\'s obligations under this Section 14.6 do not extend to: (i) Aggregated Data, which Vendor may retain and use in perpetuity in accordance with Section 5.4; (ii) Derivative Data, which is the property of Vendor under Section 6.1 and Section 6.2; or (iii) copies of Customer Data that Vendor is required to retain by applicable law, regulation, or legal hold, provided Vendor shall continue to protect such retained data in accordance with this Agreement and the BAA.

**14.7** **Survival of Aggregated Data Rights.** For the avoidance of doubt, Vendor\'s rights to use Aggregated Data and Derivative Data under Sections 5.4, 6.1, and 6.2 shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement in perpetuity, without limitation. The survival of such rights is a material element of the consideration exchanged under this Agreement and shall not be affected by the manner in which this Agreement is terminated.

Best Company:
