**[5. Confidentiality and Intellectual Property Protections]{.underline}**

Langford recognizes that the ClinIQ™ platform represents a significant investment in proprietary research and development by Cognitive Foundry, and that the audit process necessarily involves exposure to sensitive proprietary information. Langford is committed to implementing robust confidentiality and intellectual property protections that safeguard Cognitive Foundry\'s proprietary interests while enabling Langford to perform rigorous, independent audit work. The following sections describe the confidentiality framework Langford proposes for the engagement.

**5.1 Non-Disclosure Agreement**

Langford\'s standard engagement protocol requires execution of a comprehensive non-disclosure agreement (\"NDA\") between Langford and Cognitive Foundry prior to any access by Langford personnel to Cognitive Foundry\'s proprietary materials. This NDA will be a condition precedent to the commencement of any Tier 2 or Tier 3 audit activities.

The scope of the NDA will cover all model architecture documentation, training data provenance information, validation datasets, model weights, embedding layers, vector representations, training pipeline code, and any other proprietary technical information disclosed by Cognitive Foundry to Langford during the course of any audit conducted pursuant to the engagement. The NDA will also cover any derivative information, analyses, notes, or observations generated by Langford personnel based on their review of Cognitive Foundry proprietary materials.

Langford proposes that the NDA survive termination of the audit engagement for a period of five (5) years following completion of the final audit conducted under the engagement. This survival period reflects the sensitivity and commercial value of the proprietary information involved and is intended to provide Cognitive Foundry with long-term assurance that its proprietary materials will remain protected. Langford is open to discussing the appropriate survival period with Cognitive Foundry\'s legal counsel and recognizes that the final NDA terms will be the product of negotiation among the parties.

Langford further proposes that the NDA be structured as a tripartite agreement among Langford, Meridian, and Cognitive Foundry, ensuring that all parties\' rights and obligations with respect to confidential information are clearly defined and enforceable.

**5.2 Secure-Environment-Only Access**

All audit activities requiring access to Cognitive Foundry proprietary materials --- including all Tier 2 and Tier 3 audit work --- will be conducted exclusively within Cognitive Foundry\'s designated secure environment. This secure environment may take the form of an on-site secure facility maintained by Cognitive Foundry or a Cognitive Foundry-controlled secure virtual environment accessible to Langford auditors via approved remote access protocols.

No data, model artifacts, model weights, embeddings, architecture documents, training pipeline code, or any derivative materials derived from Cognitive Foundry proprietary information may leave Cognitive Foundry\'s secure environment under any circumstances. This prohibition applies to all media and formats, including electronic copies, printouts, photographs, screenshots, handwritten notes transcribing proprietary information, and any other form of reproduction or extraction.

Langford auditors will access proprietary materials either on-site at Cognitive Foundry\'s designated facility or via Cognitive Foundry-controlled secure virtual desktop infrastructure. No local copies, downloads, screenshots, screen recordings, or reproductions of any kind are permitted. Langford auditors will not use personal devices, personal storage media, or personal cloud accounts during audit activities conducted within the secure environment.

Audit workstations and computing resources used for Tier 2 and Tier 3 audit activities will be provided or approved by Cognitive Foundry. Langford auditors will use only Cognitive Foundry-approved hardware and software for all work conducted within the secure environment. Cognitive Foundry will retain the right to monitor activity within the secure environment and to log all auditor access to proprietary materials.

**5.3 Audit Report Distribution**

Langford\'s audit reports, including annual comprehensive audit reports, semi-annual monitoring reports, and any ad hoc audit reports, will be shared only with Meridian Health Systems, Inc. (as the Customer and the party that has engaged Langford) and Cognitive Foundry, Inc. (as the Vendor and the developer of the ClinIQ™ platform). No copies of any audit report will be provided to any third party, except as required by applicable law or regulation.

The primary exception to the foregoing restriction arises under the NJ AI in Healthcare Transparency Act, which requires that audit reports be made available to the New Jersey Department of Health upon request. Langford acknowledges this regulatory requirement and will prepare audit reports in a format suitable for production to the New Jersey Department of Health. However, any regulatory disclosure of audit reports will be made by Meridian (not by Langford directly, unless otherwise directed by Meridian), and will be preceded by advance written notice to both Meridian and Cognitive Foundry to the extent permitted by applicable law. This advance notice requirement is intended to provide Cognitive Foundry with an opportunity to seek protective treatment for proprietary information contained in the audit report, to the extent such treatment is available under applicable law and regulation.

Langford will work with Meridian and Cognitive Foundry to develop audit report formats that minimize the inclusion of proprietary technical details while providing sufficient information to satisfy regulatory requirements. Where appropriate, Langford may prepare redacted versions of audit reports for regulatory production, subject to the parties\' agreement on the appropriate scope of redaction.

**5.4 Work Product Destruction**

Following delivery of the final audit report for each audit cycle to both Meridian and Cognitive Foundry, all Langford work product, notes, interim analyses, working papers, data extracts, test results, and other materials generated during the audit process will be destroyed. This destruction obligation applies to all materials in all formats, including electronic files, printed documents, handwritten notes, and any other tangible or intangible embodiments of audit work product.

Destruction will be verified by written certification from Langford to both Meridian and Cognitive Foundry, confirming that all materials subject to the destruction obligation have been destroyed in accordance with this protocol. The written certification will be delivered within thirty (30) calendar days following delivery of the final audit report for the applicable audit cycle.

Langford requests one limited exception to the destruction obligation: Langford may retain a single archival copy of the final delivered audit report (in the form delivered to Meridian and Cognitive Foundry) for Langford\'s professional records and regulatory compliance purposes. This archival copy will not include any Cognitive Foundry proprietary materials beyond what is contained in the final delivered report, and will remain subject to the ongoing NDA obligations described in Section 5.1. The purpose of this retention is to enable Langford to demonstrate the scope and quality of its professional work in the event of a professional liability inquiry, regulatory examination of Langford\'s practice, or similar circumstance requiring Langford to produce evidence of its audit activities.

**[6. Proposed Audit Cadence]{.underline}**

Langford proposes the following audit cadence for the ClinIQ™ engagement, designed to satisfy the NJ AI in Healthcare Transparency Act\'s annual audit requirement while also providing Meridian with continuous monitoring capability between comprehensive audits.

**6.1 Annual Comprehensive Audit**

Langford will conduct one (1) comprehensive audit per calendar year, conducted at the Tier 2 level (Standard Audit) as the baseline. The annual comprehensive audit will encompass all six audit components described in Section 3 of this proposal: algorithmic bias and fairness assessment, clinical accuracy validation, model drift detection and performance degradation monitoring, input data quality assessment, explainability and interpretability analysis, and regulatory compliance verification.

In the event that the triggering conditions described in Section 4.3 are met at the time of the annual comprehensive audit, the Tier 3 (Deep Audit) level may be substituted for the standard Tier 2 audit, providing maximum-depth scrutiny for that audit cycle. However, Tier 3 substitution is not the default and will be invoked only when circumstances warrant.

Langford recommends that the first annual comprehensive audit cycle commence no later than ninety (90) days following the January 6, 2026 target go-live date of the ClinIQ™ platform at Meridian facilities. This timing ensures that the initial audit occurs early enough in the deployment to identify any issues arising during the transition from testing to production operations, while allowing sufficient time for the system to accumulate a meaningful volume of production data for analysis. Subsequent annual audits should be timed to complete before the anniversary of go-live, ensuring continuous audit coverage without gaps.

The annual comprehensive audit report will be delivered to Meridian and Cognitive Foundry within forty-five (45) business days of audit commencement. This timeline assumes timely provision of required access and documentation by Cognitive Foundry, as discussed in Section 8.2.

**6.2 Semi-Annual Monitoring Reports (Tier 1)**

In addition to the annual comprehensive audit, Langford will prepare two (2) Tier 1 Summary Audit monitoring reports per calendar year, spaced approximately six months apart, with one report falling between consecutive annual comprehensive audits. The monitoring reports are designed to provide Meridian with ongoing visibility into ClinIQ™ performance trends, potential drift indicators, and emerging issues that may warrant further investigation at the next comprehensive audit or, if sufficiently urgent, an ad hoc audit.

The semi-annual monitoring reports will focus on trend analysis (identifying changes in model performance metrics over time), drift detection (flagging potential model drift using the statistical process control methods described in Section 3.3), and ongoing performance metric review (assessing whether the system continues to meet established performance thresholds). Monitoring reports will also note any significant changes to the ClinIQ™ platform, such as model updates, software releases, or changes to the data integration pipeline, that may warrant additional scrutiny.

Tier 1 monitoring reports will be delivered to Meridian and Cognitive Foundry within fifteen (15) business days of the completion of data collection for the reporting period.

**6.3 Ad Hoc Audits**

Tier 3 (Deep Audit) or supplemental Tier 2 (Standard Audit) audits may be conducted on an ad hoc basis upon the occurrence of any of the triggering events described in Section 4.3. Ad hoc audits are not scheduled in advance and are initiated in response to specific events or findings that warrant immediate or near-immediate attention.

Upon engagement for an ad hoc audit, Langford will mobilize its audit team within ten (10) business days. Langford maintains a dedicated healthcare AI audit team with the capacity to respond to urgent engagements on an expedited basis. The specific scope, timeline, and deliverables for each ad hoc audit will be determined at the time of engagement based on the nature of the triggering event and the level of access required.

**[7. Estimated Costs]{.underline}**

The following cost estimates are based on Langford\'s experience with comparable healthcare AI audit engagements and reflect the anticipated scope, complexity, and access requirements of the ClinIQ™ audit. All estimates are preliminary and subject to adjustment based on the final negotiated scope, access arrangements, and any changes to the definitive agreement between Meridian and Cognitive Foundry.

**7.1 Annual Comprehensive Audit**

**Tier 2 (Standard Audit):** The estimated cost for each annual comprehensive audit conducted at the Tier 2 level is \$180,000 to \$220,000. The range reflects variability in scope complexity, the number of ClinIQ™ modules assessed in any given audit cycle (if the platform adds or retires modules over time), the volume of test queries submitted and analyzed, and the complexity of the bias and fairness analysis required based on Meridian\'s patient demographics. The cost estimate is inclusive of all auditor personnel costs, travel expenses (for on-site access to Cognitive Foundry\'s secure environment, if required), secure-environment access time, report preparation, quality review, and one round of follow-up inquiries with Cognitive Foundry or Meridian following delivery of the draft report.

**Tier 3 (Deep Audit):** The estimated cost for each annual comprehensive audit conducted at the Tier 3 level (in lieu of a Tier 2 audit, when triggering conditions are met) is \$240,000 to \$280,000. The higher cost reflects the additional complexity and time requirements associated with secure-enclave access to model weights and training pipeline code, the specialized analytical techniques required for model-weight analysis, and the extended auditor engagement time within the secure environment. Tier 3 audits also typically require more senior audit personnel with specialized expertise in deep-learning model internals, which is reflected in the cost estimate.

Both Tier 2 and Tier 3 cost estimates include auditor travel (where applicable), secure-environment access time, report preparation, and one round of follow-up inquiries.

**7.2 Semi-Annual Tier 1 Monitoring Reports**

The estimated cost for each Tier 1 monitoring report is \$35,000 to \$50,000. This estimate reflects the limited access requirements of Tier 1 audits (no on-site or secure-environment access required) and the focused scope of the monitoring report (trend analysis, drift detection, and metric review). The range accounts for variability in the volume of output log data to be analyzed and the complexity of any emerging issues identified during the monitoring period.

With two Tier 1 monitoring reports per calendar year, the estimated annual monitoring cost is \$70,000 to \$100,000.

**7.3 Ad Hoc Audits**

Ad hoc audits --- whether conducted at the Tier 2 or Tier 3 level --- will be priced on a time-and-materials basis at Langford\'s standard professional rates. A specific cost estimate will be provided upon engagement for each ad hoc audit, based on the anticipated scope, tier, and duration of the engagement. Based on Langford\'s experience with comparable engagements, the typical cost range for an ad hoc audit is \$120,000 to \$280,000, depending on whether the audit is conducted at the Tier 2 or Tier 3 level and the complexity of the issue under investigation.

**7.4 Total Estimated Annual Cost**

The total estimated annual cost for the ClinIQ™ audit program depends on the tier level of the annual comprehensive audit and the number of ad hoc audits conducted (if any):

> **Scenario A --- Routine Year (No Ad Hoc Audits):** One Tier 2 comprehensive audit plus two Tier 1 monitoring reports: approximately **\$250,000 to \$320,000** per year. This represents the expected annual cost in a year with no cause-based triggers or ad hoc audit requirements.
>
> **Scenario B --- Cause-Based Year (No Ad Hoc Audits Beyond Comprehensive):** One Tier 3 comprehensive audit (substituted for Tier 2 based on triggering conditions) plus two Tier 1 monitoring reports: approximately **\$310,000 to \$380,000** per year.

Ad hoc audits, if conducted, would be additive to the above estimates. Langford notes that the occurrence of triggering events necessitating ad hoc audits cannot be predicted in advance, and the associated costs will depend on the specific circumstances of each event.

All cost estimates are subject to adjustment based on the final negotiated scope, access arrangements, and any changes to the terms of the definitive agreement between Meridian and Cognitive Foundry. In particular, changes to the number of ClinIQ™ modules subject to audit, the number of Meridian facilities covered, or the access protocols governing Tier 2 and Tier 3 audits may materially affect the cost estimates set forth herein.

**[8. Engagement Structure and Independence]{.underline}**

**8.1 Engagement Relationship**

Langford will be engaged by and will report directly to Meridian Health Systems, Inc. Meridian is the contracting party for purposes of the audit engagement, and Langford\'s primary reporting obligation is to Meridian. Audit reports, findings, recommendations, and all other deliverables will be delivered to Meridian in the first instance, with copies provided to Cognitive Foundry as described in Section 5.3.

Langford maintains full and complete independence from Cognitive Foundry, Inc. Langford has no prior or current advisory, consulting, investment, licensing, revenue-sharing, or other commercial relationship with Cognitive Foundry that could impair or create the appearance of impairing Langford\'s objectivity. No Langford partner, employee, or contractor has any financial interest in Cognitive Foundry or any of its affiliates. Langford\'s independence from Cognitive Foundry is essential to the credibility and regulatory value of the audit, and Langford will take all necessary steps to maintain this independence throughout the engagement.

Langford\'s audit opinions, findings, and recommendations are independent and are not subject to approval, modification, or veto by either Meridian or Cognitive Foundry. While Langford welcomes input and feedback from both parties during the audit process --- particularly with respect to factual accuracy and context --- the substantive conclusions of the audit are Langford\'s alone. This principle of independent professional judgment is fundamental to the value of the audit and to compliance with the NJ AI in Healthcare Transparency Act\'s requirement for independent auditing.

**8.2 Cooperation Requirements**

This proposal assumes that the definitive agreement between Meridian and Cognitive Foundry will include a contractual obligation on the part of Cognitive Foundry to cooperate with Langford audits. Effective audit work depends on timely provision of access, documentation, and personnel availability by the entity whose systems and processes are under review. Without a contractual cooperation obligation binding on Cognitive Foundry, Langford\'s ability to conduct meaningful, independent audits --- particularly at the Tier 2 and Tier 3 levels --- would be materially impaired.

Langford will provide Cognitive Foundry with reasonable advance notice prior to the commencement of any audit. For scheduled audits (i.e., the annual comprehensive audit and semi-annual monitoring reports), Langford proposes a minimum advance notice period of twenty (20) business days. For ad hoc Tier 3 audits triggered by the conditions described in Section 4.3, Langford proposes a minimum advance notice period of five (5) business days, recognizing that the urgency of cause-based audits may necessitate a shorter preparation window.

Langford further requests that Cognitive Foundry designate a technical liaison for each audit engagement. The designated technical liaison should have sufficient seniority and technical knowledge to facilitate Langford\'s access to required materials, respond to technical inquiries during the audit, and coordinate with Cognitive Foundry\'s engineering and data science teams as needed. The availability and responsiveness of the designated technical liaison is a material factor in Langford\'s ability to complete audits within the proposed timelines.

**8.3 Conflict of Interest**

Langford confirms that, as of the date of this proposal, it has no current conflicts of interest with respect to either Meridian Health Systems, Inc. or Cognitive Foundry, Inc. No Langford partner, employee, contractor, or affiliate has any financial, commercial, familial, or other relationship with either party that could create an actual or apparent conflict of interest.

Langford will promptly disclose to Meridian any potential conflicts of interest that may arise during the term of the engagement, including any new client relationships, business opportunities, or personnel changes that could affect Langford\'s independence or objectivity. In the event that a material conflict of interest arises that cannot be mitigated through appropriate safeguards, Langford will work with Meridian to determine the appropriate course of action, which may include engagement of a substitute auditor.

**[9. Assumptions and Limitations]{.underline}**

This proposal is preliminary in nature and is subject to the following assumptions and limitations:

1\. **Contractual Access Provisions.** This proposal assumes that audit access provisions --- including the tiered access framework described in Section 4, the confidentiality protections described in Section 5, and the cooperation requirements described in Section 8.2 --- will be incorporated into the definitive agreement between Meridian and Cognitive Foundry governing the ClinIQ™ platform deployment. The feasibility and scope of Langford\'s audit engagement depend materially on the access provisions ultimately negotiated and agreed upon by Meridian and Cognitive Foundry.

2\. **Legal Review.** The scope and methodology proposed herein are subject to review and refinement by Meridian\'s legal counsel, Whitfield Ames LLP, and Cognitive Foundry\'s legal counsel, Kessler Pratt & Lowe LLP. Langford anticipates that legal review may result in modifications to the proposed tier definitions, triggering conditions, confidentiality provisions, or other elements of this proposal. Langford is prepared to work collaboratively with counsel for both parties to arrive at an audit framework that is acceptable to all stakeholders.

3\. **Cost Estimate Basis.** The cost estimates set forth in Section 7 are based on Langford\'s experience with comparable healthcare AI platform audits and reflect Langford\'s current understanding of the ClinIQ™ platform\'s scope, complexity, and deployment architecture. These estimates are subject to adjustment based on the final negotiated access terms, changes in the number of ClinIQ™ modules subject to audit, changes in the number of Meridian facilities covered, and any other scope modifications that emerge from the negotiation process.

4\. **Tier 3 Secure Enclave.** The Tier 3 access cost estimates assume that Cognitive Foundry will provide a functional secure enclave environment that meets Langford\'s audit requirements, including adequate computing resources, appropriate software tools, and reliable network connectivity. If Langford is required to establish, configure, or supplement the secure-access infrastructure --- for example, by providing specialized audit tools, additional computing resources, or custom analytical environments --- additional costs may apply and will be estimated at the time of engagement.

5\. **Non-Binding Nature.** This proposal does not constitute a binding offer of services, a binding engagement agreement, or a commitment by Langford to perform audit services at the rates or on the terms described herein. Formal engagement of Langford will require execution of an engagement letter between Langford and Meridian, and, as applicable, execution of a tripartite NDA among Langford, Meridian, and Cognitive Foundry. The terms of the engagement letter and NDA will govern the parties\' rights and obligations with respect to the audit engagement.

6\. **Regulatory Changes.** This proposal is based on the regulatory landscape as of the date hereof. Material changes to applicable federal or state laws or regulations --- including amendments to the NJ AI in Healthcare Transparency Act, new FDA guidance on AI/ML-based clinical decision-support software, or the enactment of AI-specific legislation in Delaware or Pennsylvania --- may necessitate modifications to the proposed audit scope and methodology.

**[10. Next Steps]{.underline}**

Langford proposes the following next steps to advance discussions regarding the audit engagement:

1\. **Stakeholder Meeting.** Langford requests a meeting with Dr. Priya Narayanan, Senior Vice President and General Counsel of Meridian, and Marcus Delgado, Vice President of Digital Health and Technology Transactions at Meridian, to discuss this proposal in detail, address questions, and refine the proposed audit scope and methodology based on Meridian\'s priorities and requirements. Langford is available to conduct this meeting at Meridian\'s convenience, either in person at Meridian\'s offices or via videoconference.

2\. **Tripartite Discussions.** Langford is available to participate in discussions with Cognitive Foundry regarding the Tier 2 and Tier 3 access protocols, the secure-environment specifications, and the confidentiality and intellectual property protection arrangements described in this proposal. Langford understands that Cognitive Foundry\'s willingness to provide the levels of access described herein is a critical prerequisite to the engagement and is prepared to work constructively with Cognitive Foundry to address any concerns regarding intellectual property protection.

3\. **Engagement Finalization.** Langford is prepared to finalize engagement terms, including execution of an engagement letter and tripartite NDA, in coordination with the target execution date of August 15, 2025 for the definitive agreement between Meridian and Cognitive Foundry governing the ClinIQ™ platform deployment. Aligning the audit engagement timeline with the definitive agreement timeline will ensure that Langford is fully operational and audit-ready by the January 6, 2026 target go-live date for the ClinIQ™ platform at Meridian facilities, and that the first annual comprehensive audit can commence within ninety (90) days of go-live as recommended in Section 6.1.

**[11. Contact Information]{.underline}**

For questions, comments, or follow-up discussions regarding this proposal, please contact:

**Dr. Anil Cherian, Managing Director** Langford AI Assurance Group, LLC 101 Federal Street, Suite 1900 Boston, MA 02110

Telephone: (617) 555-0194

Email: a.cherian@langfordaiassurance.com

 

 

*This document is a preliminary proposal for discussion purposes only and does not constitute a binding offer of services. Langford AI Assurance Group, LLC reserves the right to modify the proposed scope, methodology, and cost estimates based on the outcome of negotiations among the parties and final contractual access provisions. The information contained herein is confidential and is intended solely for the use of Meridian Health Systems, Inc. and its authorized representatives. Any unauthorized distribution, reproduction, or use of this document is strictly prohibited.*

**[EXHIBIT D]{.underline}**

**[DATA GOVERNANCE AND USAGE TERMS]{.underline}**

**to the Master AI Services Agreement**

**between**

**MERIDIAN HEALTH SYSTEMS, INC.** a Delaware corporation (\"Customer\" or \"Meridian\")

**and**

**COGNITIVE FOUNDRY, INC.** a Delaware corporation (\"Service Provider\" or \"Cognitive Foundry\")

*Draft dated June 6, 2025*

CONFIDENTIAL --- SUBJECT TO ONGOING NEGOTIATION --- BRACKETED ALTERNATIVES REMAIN OPEN FOR RESOLUTION AT JOINT NEGOTIATION SESSION SCHEDULED JULY 18, 2025

Right-click to update Table of Contents

**[Section 1 --- Purpose and Scope]{.underline}**

**1.1 Purpose.** The purpose of this Exhibit D (this \"Exhibit\") is to establish a comprehensive data governance framework governing all data received, created, processed, stored, transmitted, or otherwise handled by Cognitive Foundry, Inc. (\"Cognitive Foundry\") in connection with the ClinIQ™ Platform (v4.2) services provided to Meridian Health Systems, Inc. (\"Meridian\") under the Master AI Services Agreement dated as of even date herewith, by and between Meridian and Cognitive Foundry (as amended, restated, supplemented, or otherwise modified from time to time, the \"Agreement\"). This Exhibit sets forth the parties\' respective rights, obligations, and restrictions with respect to the classification, handling, use, retention, return, destruction, and portability of all such data, and establishes the security, regulatory compliance, and audit requirements applicable thereto.

**1.2 Scope --- Covered Facilities and Systems.** This Exhibit applies to all data flowing between or among Meridian\'s fourteen (14) covered facilities located across the States of Delaware, Pennsylvania, and New Jersey, the MeridianConnect electronic health record (\"EHR\") platform (powered by Epic Systems Corporation), and the ClinIQ™ Platform hosted on Amazon Web Services GovCloud (us-east-1 region). The covered facilities include all hospitals, ambulatory care centers, outpatient clinics, and ancillary service locations operated by Meridian or its affiliates that are identified in Exhibit A to the Agreement as of the date hereof, or that are subsequently added to the scope of the Agreement by written amendment or change order.

**1.3 Scope --- Users and Encounter Volume.** This Exhibit applies to all data associated with the six thousand two hundred (6,200) Named Licensed Users authorized to access the ClinIQ™ Platform under the Agreement, and to data arising from or related to the approximately two million one hundred thousand (2,100,000) annual patient encounters occurring across Meridian\'s covered facilities. The foregoing encounter volume is provided for planning and scoping purposes; the obligations of the parties under this Exhibit shall apply regardless of fluctuations in actual encounter volume.

**1.4 Hierarchy of Control.** In the event of any conflict, inconsistency, or ambiguity between the provisions of this Exhibit D and the body of the Agreement, the provision that affords greater protection to Customer Data (as defined in Section 2 below) shall govern and control. In the event of any conflict, inconsistency, or ambiguity between this Exhibit D and the Business Associate Agreement attached as Exhibit F to the Agreement (executed June 2, 2025) (the \"BAA\"), the BAA shall govern and control with respect to obligations specifically relating to Protected Health Information (\"PHI\"), and this Exhibit D shall govern all other data governance matters not specifically addressed by the BAA. Nothing in this Exhibit D is intended to diminish, limit, or supersede the protections afforded to PHI under the BAA, HIPAA, or the HITECH Act.

**[Section 2 --- Definitions]{.underline}**

For purposes of this Exhibit D, the following terms shall have the meanings set forth below. Capitalized terms used but not defined in this Section 2 shall have the meanings assigned to them in the Agreement or, with respect to PHI-related terms, in the BAA (Exhibit F).

**2.1 \"Customer Data\"** means all data provided by or on behalf of Meridian to Cognitive Foundry, or generated, derived, collected, or created by the ClinIQ™ Platform in connection with Meridian\'s use thereof, including without limitation: (a) Protected Health Information (\"PHI\"); (b) clinical records, including patient charts, lab results, diagnostic imaging metadata, progress notes, discharge summaries, and clinical decision-support outputs; (c) operational data, including staffing data, scheduling information, bed-management records, and supply-chain data processed through or by the ClinIQ™ Platform; (d) user-interaction logs, including clickstream data, search queries, feature-usage metrics, session recordings, and user-preference settings; (e) configuration data, including workflow rules, template configurations, alert thresholds, and integration parameters; and (f) any data output generated by the ClinIQ™ Platform at the direction of or on behalf of Meridian or its authorized users, including reports, dashboards, analytics, predictions, risk scores, and any other output produced by any artificial intelligence or machine learning model within the ClinIQ™ Platform in response to Customer Data inputs.

**2.2 \"Protected Health Information\" or \"PHI\"** has the meaning set forth in 45 C.F.R. § 160.103, as amended from time to time, and as further specified and supplemented in the BAA (Exhibit F). For the avoidance of doubt, PHI includes electronic protected health information (\"ePHI\") as defined in 45 C.F.R. § 160.103.

**2.3 \"Aggregated Data\"** means statistical compilations, summary metrics, trend analyses, population-health analytics, or other data sets derived from Customer Data that satisfy each of the following requirements: (a) the data set contains no individual-level PHI; (b) the data set cannot be used, alone or in combination with other information reasonably available to Cognitive Foundry or any recipient, to identify any individual patient, healthcare provider, or Meridian employee; and (c) the data set has been combined with data from at least two (2) other unrelated sources (i.e., sources other than Meridian or Meridian\'s affiliates) such that the Meridian-specific contribution to the data set is not separately extractable, identifiable, or reverse-engineerable through commercially reasonable means. The burden of demonstrating that data qualifies as Aggregated Data rests with Cognitive Foundry.

**2.4 \"De-Identified Data\"** means data derived from Customer Data that satisfies either of the following standards, as established under the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996 and its implementing regulations:

> \(a\) **Safe Harbor Method (45 C.F.R. § 164.514(b)(2)):** all eighteen (18) categories of identifiers specified in 45 C.F.R. § 164.514(b)(2)(i) have been removed or generalized to the extent required by the regulation, and the covered entity or business associate does not have actual knowledge that the remaining information could be used, alone or in combination with other information, to identify an individual who is a subject of the information; or
>
> \(b\) **Expert Determination Method (45 C.F.R. § 164.514(b)(1)):** a person with appropriate knowledge of and experience with generally accepted statistical and scientific principles and methods for rendering information not individually identifiable has applied such principles and methods, has determined that the risk is very small that the information could be used, alone or in combination with other reasonably available information, by an anticipated recipient to identify an individual who is a subject of the information, and has documented the methods and results of the analysis that justify such determination.

The burden of demonstrating that data qualifies as De-Identified Data, and of maintaining documentation supporting such qualification, rests with Cognitive Foundry.

**2.5 \"Model Training Data\"** means any subset of Customer Data, Aggregated Data, De-Identified Data, or any combination thereof, that is used or proposed to be used by Cognitive Foundry for the training, fine-tuning, validation, testing, benchmarking, evaluation, reinforcement, or improvement of any artificial intelligence model, machine learning model, neural network, algorithm, or other computational model, whether or not such model is part of the ClinIQ™ Platform or any other Cognitive Foundry product or service.

**2.6 \"Interoperable Formats\"** means FHIR R4 (Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources, Release 4, as published by Health Level Seven International) and HL7 v2 (Health Level Seven Version 2.x messaging standards), or such successor standards as are then-current and in widespread use in the United States healthcare industry at the time of the applicable data export or transfer. References to Interoperable Formats shall be construed to include all profiles, implementation guides, and extensions necessary for compatibility with the MeridianConnect (Epic Systems Corporation) EHR platform or any successor EHR system designated by Meridian.

**2.7 \"Derived Data\"** means, collectively, Aggregated Data and De-Identified Data, as those terms are defined in Sections 2.3 and 2.4, respectively.

**2.8 Cross-References.** Capitalized terms used but not otherwise defined in this Exhibit D shall have the meanings assigned to such terms in the Agreement or, with respect to PHI-specific terms, in the BAA (Exhibit F). In the event of any conflict between a definition in this Exhibit D and a definition in the Agreement or the BAA, the definition providing the broadest scope of protection for Customer Data or PHI, as applicable, shall control.

**[Section 3 --- Data Classification Framework]{.underline}**

**Section 3.1 --- Classification Tiers**

All data within the scope of this Exhibit D shall be classified into one of the following four (4) tiers, in descending order of sensitivity. The classification of a given data element shall determine the security controls, access restrictions, usage rights, and regulatory compliance obligations applicable to such data element.

**Tier 1 --- Protected Health Information (PHI).** Tier 1 is the highest sensitivity classification and encompasses all PHI, as defined in Section 2.2 of this Exhibit D. Data classified as Tier 1 is subject to: (a) all requirements of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996 (42 U.S.C. § 1320d et seq.), as amended by the Health Information Technology for Economic and Clinical Health Act (42 U.S.C. § 17931 et seq.) (collectively, \"HIPAA/HITECH\"), and their implementing regulations; (b) all obligations of the BAA (Exhibit F, executed June 2, 2025); (c) all applicable state health-information-privacy laws, including without limitation the laws of the States of Delaware, Pennsylvania, and New Jersey; (d) the New Jersey Artificial Intelligence in Healthcare Transparency Act (effective March 1, 2025) (the \"NJ AI Act\"), with respect to Meridian\'s three (3) New Jersey facilities (Meridian Atlantic Medical Center, Meridian South Jersey Regional Hospital, and Meridian Princeton Medical Center); and (e) the encryption, access-control, audit-logging, and breach-notification requirements set forth in Section 4 of this Exhibit D. Tier 1 data may not be used for any purpose other than the performance of services under the Agreement, compliance with legal obligations, and such other purposes as are expressly authorized by the BAA (Exhibit F) and applicable law.

**Tier 2 --- Customer Data (Non-PHI).** Tier 2 encompasses all Customer Data that does not constitute PHI, including but not limited to operational data, usage analytics, system configuration data, administrative records, user-interaction logs (to the extent not containing PHI), and platform-performance metrics. Tier 2 data is owned exclusively by Meridian and is subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in the Agreement and this Exhibit D. Access to Tier 2 data by Cognitive Foundry personnel is restricted to those individuals with a documented need-to-know for the performance of services under the Agreement.

**Tier 3 --- Aggregated Data.** Tier 3 encompasses data that qualifies as Aggregated Data under the definition set forth in Section 2.3 of this Exhibit D. Tier 3 data is derived data that has been stripped of individual-level PHI, cannot be used to identify any individual, and has been combined with data from at least two (2) other unrelated sources such that the Meridian-specific contribution is not separately extractable. Usage rights for Tier 3 data are governed by Section 5.2 of this Exhibit D, which currently contains contested bracketed alternatives to be resolved by the parties.

**Tier 4 --- De-Identified Data.** Tier 4 encompasses data that qualifies as De-Identified Data under the definition set forth in Section 2.4 of this Exhibit D, meeting either the Safe Harbor standard under 45 C.F.R. § 164.514(b)(2) or the Expert Determination standard under 45 C.F.R. § 164.514(b)(1). Usage rights for Tier 4 data are governed by Section 5.2 of this Exhibit D, which currently contains contested bracketed alternatives to be resolved by the parties.

**Section 3.2 --- Classification Responsibility**

**3.2.1 Automated Classification.** Cognitive Foundry shall design, implement, and maintain automated classification tagging functionality within the ClinIQ™ Platform to label all ingested, generated, derived, and received data by the applicable tier classification at the point of creation, receipt, or ingestion. Such automated classification shall operate in real-time or near-real-time and shall apply consistent, auditable logic that is documented and made available to Meridian upon request. Cognitive Foundry shall conduct quarterly validation testing of its automated classification system and shall promptly remediate any identified errors or misclassifications.

**3.2.2 Meridian Reclassification Authority.** Notwithstanding the automated classification functionality described in Section 3.2.1, Meridian retains final, binding authority to reclassify any data element to a higher or lower tier upon written notice to Cognitive Foundry. Cognitive Foundry shall implement any such reclassification within five (5) business days of receipt of Meridian\'s written notice, including the application of all security controls, access restrictions, and usage limitations applicable to the newly assigned tier. During the period between Meridian\'s reclassification notice and Cognitive Foundry\'s implementation thereof, the data element in question shall be treated in accordance with whichever tier affords the greater level of protection.

**3.2.3 Dispute Escalation.** Any dispute between the parties regarding the proper classification of a data element shall be escalated promptly to the following designated representatives for resolution:

> For Meridian: Marcus Delgado, Vice President, Digital Health & Technology Transactions
>
> For Cognitive Foundry: Sandra Hu, Vice President, Commercial Legal

The designated representatives shall confer in good faith and use commercially reasonable efforts to resolve the classification dispute within ten (10) business days of escalation. If the dispute cannot be resolved within such period, it shall be further escalated in accordance with the dispute resolution procedures set forth in the Agreement. Pending resolution of any classification dispute, the data element in question shall be treated in accordance with the higher (more restrictive) of the proposed tier classifications.

**Section 3.3 --- De-Identification Certification**

**3.3.1 Methodology Requirements.** Any Customer Data proposed for de-identification by Cognitive Foundry must be processed using a documented, repeatable, and auditable methodology that is consistent with current best practices in statistical disclosure limitation and that satisfies the requirements of either 45 C.F.R. § 164.514(b)(1) (Expert Determination) or 45 C.F.R. § 164.514(b)(2) (Safe Harbor), as applicable. Cognitive Foundry shall maintain written documentation of the de-identification methodology, including all algorithms, transformations, generalization rules, suppression thresholds, and privacy parameters applied to the data.

**3.3.2 Expert Determination --- Independence and Pre-Approval.** For any de-identification conducted pursuant to the Expert Determination method under 45 C.F.R. § 164.514(b)(1), the certifying statistical expert must be: (a) independent of Cognitive Foundry, meaning not an employee, officer, director, or equity holder of Cognitive Foundry, and not engaged by Cognitive Foundry in any other capacity that could reasonably be perceived as creating a conflict of interest; and (b) approved in advance by Meridian. As of the date of this Exhibit D, Langford AI Assurance Group, LLC, under the direction of Dr. Anil Cherian, Managing Director, is a pre-approved certifying party for purposes of this Section 3.3.2. Meridian may approve additional certifying parties from time to time upon written notice to Cognitive Foundry.

**3.3.3 Certification Deliverables.** Prior to any use of De-Identified Data resulting from a de-identification process conducted by or on behalf of Cognitive Foundry, Cognitive Foundry shall provide Meridian with: (a) the written certification of the certifying expert (in the case of Expert Determination) or a compliance attestation (in the case of Safe Harbor); (b) complete methodology documentation, including all parameters and transformations applied; (c) a re-identification risk analysis quantifying the residual risk of re-identification using then-current industry-standard attack models; and (d) a data-sample review demonstrating the completeness and accuracy of the de-identification. Meridian shall have fifteen (15) business days from receipt of all deliverables to review and either approve or object to the proposed de-identification.

**3.3.4 Prohibition on Re-Identification.** Cognitive Foundry shall not attempt to re-identify, reverse-engineer, or otherwise derive individually identifiable information from any De-Identified Data, nor shall Cognitive Foundry permit, authorize, facilitate, or assist any third party in doing so. Cognitive Foundry shall include comparable prohibitions on re-identification in all contracts or agreements with subcontractors or downstream recipients who receive access to De-Identified Data. Any breach of this Section 3.3.4 shall constitute a material breach of this Exhibit D and the Agreement.

**[Section 4 --- PHI Handling and Security Requirements]{.underline}**

**Section 4.1 --- Cross-Reference to BAA**

All obligations of Cognitive Foundry regarding the handling, use, disclosure, safeguarding, and disposition of PHI are subject to and governed by the Business Associate Agreement attached as Exhibit F to the Agreement, executed by the parties on June 2, 2025. The BAA establishes the primary framework for PHI compliance, including permitted uses and disclosures, individual rights, breach notification, and regulatory cooperation. Cognitive Foundry\'s obligations under this Section 4 are in addition to, and do not limit, reduce, or supersede, Cognitive Foundry\'s obligations under the BAA (Exhibit F). In the event of any conflict between this Section 4 and the BAA, the BAA shall control with respect to PHI-specific obligations, and the provision affording greater protection to PHI shall control in all other respects.

**Section 4.2 --- Encryption Standards**

**4.2.1 Encryption at Rest.** Cognitive Foundry shall implement and maintain AES-256 (Advanced Encryption Standard, 256-bit key length) encryption for all Customer Data, including all PHI, stored within the ClinIQ™ Platform, AWS GovCloud (us-east-1 region), and any backup, archive, replica, snapshot, or disaster-recovery environment maintained by or on behalf of Cognitive Foundry. Encryption at rest shall extend to all storage media, including but not limited to block storage volumes (Amazon EBS), object storage (Amazon S3), database storage (Amazon RDS, Amazon DynamoDB, or equivalent), file systems, and any temporary or ephemeral storage used during data processing. Encryption shall be applied at the volume or object level, as appropriate, with no unencrypted copies of Customer Data permitted in any persistent storage medium.

**4.2.2 Encryption in Transit.** Cognitive Foundry shall implement and maintain TLS 1.3 (Transport Layer Security, version 1.3) as the minimum encryption protocol for all data transmissions between Meridian systems (including the MeridianConnect/Epic EHR platform), the ClinIQ™ Platform, any Cognitive Foundry-managed infrastructure, and any third-party system or service with which Customer Data is exchanged. All API endpoints, data feeds, integration interfaces, SFTP channels, and any other data-transfer mechanisms shall be configured to require TLS 1.3 or higher.

**4.2.3 Protocol Restrictions.** No downgrade to prior TLS versions (including TLS 1.2, TLS 1.1, TLS 1.0, or any version of SSL) is permitted within any component of the ClinIQ™ environment that handles, processes, stores, or transmits Customer Data. Cognitive Foundry shall affirmatively disable TLS 1.2 and all earlier protocol versions within the ClinIQ™ environment and shall monitor for and promptly remediate any configuration drift that would permit the use of deprecated protocol versions.

**Section 4.3 --- Key Management**

**4.3.1 Customer-Managed Key Escrow.** Meridian shall retain exclusive administrative control of all master encryption keys used to encrypt Customer Data (including PHI) within the ClinIQ™ Platform environment. Such control shall be exercised through a key management service (\"KMS\") instance provisioned within Meridian\'s own AWS GovCloud (us-east-1) account, or through such other FIPS 140-2 Level 3 (or successor standard) validated hardware security module (\"HSM\") solution as Meridian may designate from time to time by written notice to Cognitive Foundry. Cognitive Foundry shall configure the ClinIQ™ Platform to utilize Meridian\'s customer-managed keys for all encryption operations, and shall not maintain independent copies of master encryption keys.

**4.3.2 Access Restrictions.** Cognitive Foundry personnel shall not have standing, persistent, or default access to master encryption keys. Any access by Cognitive Foundry to master encryption keys requires Meridian\'s prior written authorization on a per-incident or per-purpose basis. All such access events shall be logged in tamper-evident audit records that include the identity of the individual accessing the key, the purpose of access, the date and time of access, and the duration of access. Meridian shall have real-time visibility into all key-access logs through a dashboard or reporting interface provided by Cognitive Foundry.

**4.3.3 Key Rotation.** Meridian shall initiate key rotation no less frequently than once per calendar year. In addition, Cognitive Foundry shall promptly notify Meridian if there is reasonable suspicion that any encryption key has been compromised, and Meridian shall have the right to initiate immediate key rotation upon such notification. Cognitive Foundry shall support and cooperate with all key-rotation activities, including re-encryption of affected data, with no disruption to the availability of the ClinIQ™ Platform services.

**Section 4.4 --- Access Controls**

**4.4.1 Role-Based Access Controls (RBAC).** Cognitive Foundry shall implement and enforce role-based access controls within the ClinIQ™ Platform, designed in accordance with the principle of least privilege. Each role shall be defined with the minimum set of permissions necessary for the performance of the specific function assigned to that role. Role definitions and associated permissions shall be documented and provided to Meridian upon request.

**4.4.2 Personnel Access Restrictions.** Access to Customer Data (including PHI) by Cognitive Foundry personnel shall be limited to those individuals who have a documented, current, and legitimate need-to-know for the performance of services under the Agreement. Each such individual must be identified by name, title, role, and scope of access in a Vendor Access Register that Cognitive Foundry shall prepare, maintain, and deliver to Meridian on a quarterly basis, no later than fifteen (15) calendar days after the end of each calendar quarter. Cognitive Foundry shall promptly update the Vendor Access Register to reflect any additions, removals, or changes in access scope.

**4.4.3 Multi-Factor Authentication.** Multi-factor authentication (\"MFA\") shall be required for all Cognitive Foundry personnel accessing any environment, system, application, database, tool, or interface that contains, processes, stores, or provides access to Customer Data. MFA shall consist of at least two (2) independent authentication factors from different categories (e.g., something the user knows, something the user has, or something the user is). Single-factor authentication shall not be permitted under any circumstances for access to Customer Data environments.

**4.4.4 Access Revocation.** Meridian retains the right to require immediate revocation of any individual\'s access to Customer Data or to any environment containing Customer Data, at any time and for any reason, upon written notice to Cognitive Foundry (which may be delivered by email to the designated security contact). Cognitive Foundry shall effectuate such revocation within four (4) hours of receipt of Meridian\'s notice during business hours, or within eight (8) hours if notice is received outside of business hours.

**Section 4.5 --- Breach Notification**

**4.5.1 BAA Notification Requirements.** In accordance with the BAA (Exhibit F), Cognitive Foundry shall notify Meridian of any Breach of Unsecured PHI (as defined in 45 C.F.R. § 164.402) within the timeframes and in the manner specified in the BAA. The notification obligations set forth in this Section 4.5 are supplemental to, and do not limit or modify, the notification obligations of the BAA.

**4.5.2 State Law Notification Requirements.** In addition to the BAA notification requirements, Cognitive Foundry shall comply with all applicable state breach-notification statutes, including without limitation: (a) the Pennsylvania Breach of Personal Information Notification Act (73 Pa. Stat. § 2303) with respect to personal information of individuals associated with Meridian\'s Pennsylvania facilities; and (b) the NJ AI in Healthcare Transparency Act (effective March 1, 2025), including any notification obligations to the New Jersey Department of Health arising from a breach or unauthorized disclosure of data associated with AI-assisted clinical decision-making at Meridian\'s three (3) New Jersey facilities.

**4.5.3 Cooperation and Cost Allocation.** Cognitive Foundry shall cooperate fully with Meridian in all breach investigation, notification, mitigation, and remediation activities, including providing timely access to relevant logs, records, personnel, and systems. To the extent that a breach arose from or was caused by the acts, omissions, negligence, or willful misconduct of Cognitive Foundry, its employees, subcontractors, or agents, Cognitive Foundry shall bear all costs and expenses associated with the breach, including but not limited to investigation costs, notification costs, credit-monitoring services, regulatory fines and penalties, and reasonable attorneys\' fees.

**Section 4.6 --- Audit and Monitoring**

**4.6.1 Audit Log Maintenance.** Cognitive Foundry shall maintain comprehensive, tamper-evident audit logs of all access to, use of, modifications to, and operations on Customer Data, including without limitation read access, write operations, data exports, data deletions, administrative actions, and configuration changes. Such audit logs shall be maintained for a minimum period of six (6) years from the date of the logged event, or such longer period as may be required by applicable law, regulation, or the data-retention schedule approved by Meridian under Section 6.1 of this Exhibit D.

**4.6.2 Meridian Audit Rights.** Meridian, or its designated auditor, may audit Cognitive Foundry\'s compliance with this Section 4 and any other provision of this Exhibit D upon thirty (30) days\' prior written notice. Scheduled audits shall be limited to no more than two (2) per calendar year. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Meridian\'s right to conduct for-cause audits --- i.e., audits triggered by a suspected breach, suspected non-compliance, regulatory investigation, or other good-faith basis for concern --- shall be unlimited in number and may be conducted upon reasonable notice (which may be shorter than thirty (30) days as circumstances warrant). Cognitive Foundry shall provide reasonable cooperation and access to facilities, systems, records, and personnel in connection with any audit.

**4.6.3 Pre-Approved Auditor.** Langford AI Assurance Group, LLC (Dr. Anil Cherian, Managing Director) is a pre-approved independent auditor for purposes of this Section 4.6 and Section 3.3 of this Exhibit D. Meridian may engage Langford AI Assurance Group, LLC to conduct audits without further approval from Cognitive Foundry. Meridian may designate additional auditors from time to time upon written notice to Cognitive Foundry, provided that such auditors are not direct competitors of Cognitive Foundry and agree to reasonable confidentiality obligations regarding Cognitive Foundry\'s proprietary systems and processes.

**[Section 5 --- Data Usage Rights]{.underline}**

**Section 5.1 --- Customer Data: Exclusive Ownership and Restricted Use**

**5.1.1 Ownership.** All Customer Data is and shall remain the sole and exclusive property of Meridian. Nothing in the Agreement, this Exhibit D, or any other agreement between the parties shall be construed as transferring, conveying, or assigning to Cognitive Foundry any right, title, or interest in or to any Customer Data. Cognitive Foundry hereby disclaims and irrevocably waives any claim of ownership, lien, or interest in Customer Data.

**5.1.2 Limited License.** Cognitive Foundry\'s right to access and use Customer Data is limited strictly to the following purposes: (a) performing the services under the Agreement in accordance with the applicable Statement of Work, service levels, and technical specifications; (b) fulfilling its obligations under this Exhibit D and the BAA (Exhibit F); and (c) complying with applicable law, including responding to lawful subpoenas, court orders, or regulatory inquiries, subject to the notice requirements set forth in Section 5.3(b) of this Exhibit D.

**5.1.3 Prohibition on Unauthorized Use.** No use of Customer Data beyond the limited purposes set forth in Section 5.1.2 is permitted without Meridian\'s prior written consent. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, Cognitive Foundry shall not use Customer Data --- in whole or in part, directly or indirectly --- for model training, fine-tuning, benchmarking, product development, competitive analysis, marketing, or any other purpose that benefits Cognitive Foundry, its affiliates, or any third party, except as expressly authorized in writing by Meridian. Any unauthorized use of Customer Data shall constitute a material breach of this Exhibit D and the Agreement.

**Section 5.2 --- Aggregated and De-Identified Data Usage**

**5.2A.2 Opt-Out Right.** Meridian may opt out of such use of Derived Data at any time by providing written notice to Cognitive Foundry. Any such opt-out shall take effect ninety (90) days after Cognitive Foundry\'s receipt of the opt-out notice (the \"Opt-Out Effective Date\").

**5.2A.3 Effect of Opt-Out.** Following the Opt-Out Effective Date, Cognitive Foundry shall cease all new uses of Meridian-derived Derived Data on a prospective basis. For the avoidance of doubt, Cognitive Foundry shall have no obligation to delete, purge, retrain, modify, or reverse the incorporation of Derived Data that has already been incorporated into models, benchmarks, or products prior to the Opt-Out Effective Date.

Cognitive Foundry shall provide Meridian with an annual summary report, delivered no later than March 31 of each calendar year, describing the categories of use to which Meridian-derived Derived Data has been applied during the preceding calendar year.

**\[ALTERNATIVE B --- MERIDIAN POSITION\]**

**5.2B.1 No Use Without Affirmative Consent.** Cognitive Foundry shall not use any Aggregated Data, De-Identified Data, or any other data derived from Customer Data (collectively, \"Derived Data\") for any purpose other than performing the services under the Agreement, unless Meridian provides affirmative prior written consent on a per-use-case basis.

**5.2B.2 Consent Request Requirements.** Each request for consent to use Derived Data shall be submitted by Cognitive Foundry in writing to Meridian\'s designated representative and shall identify with specificity: (i) the data elements and data sets to be used, including a description of the de-identification or aggregation methodology applied; (ii) the specific purpose of the proposed use, including a description of the model, product, or project for which the data will be used; (iii) the anticipated duration of the proposed use; (iv) any third parties who will receive, access, or otherwise have visibility into the Derived Data; and (v) the technical, administrative, and organizational safeguards to be applied to the Derived Data during the proposed use.

**5.2B.3 Consent Determination.** Meridian may grant, deny, condition, limit, or revoke consent at any time and in its sole and absolute discretion. No consent shall be implied by Meridian\'s silence, delay, or failure to respond to a consent request. Any consent granted by Meridian shall be limited to the specific use case described in the consent request and shall not extend to any other use, purpose, or data set.

**5.2B.4 Revocation and Deletion.** Revocation of consent shall take effect immediately upon written notice from Meridian to Cognitive Foundry. Upon revocation of consent or upon Meridian\'s request at any time, Cognitive Foundry shall perform verified deletion of all Derived Data associated with the revoked consent within thirty (30) days, regardless of whether such Derived Data has been incorporated into models, benchmarks, products, or any other system or output. Such deletion shall include the removal, retraining, or modification of any model, algorithm, or system that has incorporated or been influenced by the affected Derived Data to the extent necessary to eliminate the contribution of such data. Within five (5) business days after completion of the deletion, Cognitive Foundry shall provide Meridian with a written certification of deletion executed by an officer of Cognitive Foundry at the level of Vice President or above.

**Section 5.3 --- Prohibition on Sale or Disclosure**

Regardless of which alternative is adopted under Section 5.2, the following prohibition shall apply in all cases:

**5.3.1 General Prohibition.** Cognitive Foundry shall not sell, license, sublicense, rent, lease, transfer, distribute, publish, or otherwise disclose Customer Data, Aggregated Data, De-Identified Data, or any data derived in whole or in part from the foregoing, to any third party except as follows:

> \(a\) **Approved Subcontractors.** Cognitive Foundry may disclose Customer Data to subcontractors that have been approved in writing by Meridian in accordance with the subcontractor approval provisions of the Agreement, provided that each such subcontractor is bound by written obligations that are at least as restrictive as the obligations imposed on Cognitive Foundry under this Exhibit D, the Agreement, and the BAA (Exhibit F) with respect to the data disclosed. Cognitive Foundry shall remain fully responsible and liable for the acts and omissions of its subcontractors with respect to Customer Data.
>
> \(b\) **Legal Process.** Cognitive Foundry may disclose Customer Data as required by applicable law, regulation, or valid legal process (including a lawful subpoena, civil investigative demand, or court order), provided that Cognitive Foundry shall: (i) promptly notify Meridian in writing of the legal demand prior to making any disclosure, to the maximum extent permitted by law; (ii) cooperate with Meridian in seeking a protective order or other appropriate remedy to limit the scope of the disclosure; (iii) disclose only the minimum amount of Customer Data reasonably necessary to comply with the legal demand; and (iv) use commercially reasonable efforts to ensure that the disclosed data is treated as confidential by the recipient.
>
> \(c\) **Express Written Authorization.** Cognitive Foundry may disclose Customer Data as expressly authorized in a prior writing signed by an authorized representative of Meridian, subject to any conditions or limitations specified in such authorization.

**5.3.2 No Sale of Data.** For the avoidance of doubt, Cognitive Foundry shall not sell Customer Data as the term \"sell\" is defined under the California Consumer Privacy Act (Cal. Civ. Code § 1798.140(ad)), the Colorado Privacy Act (Colo. Rev. Stat. § 6-1-1303(23)), or any other applicable state privacy law, regardless of whether such law is directly applicable to the transactions contemplated by this Exhibit D.

**Section 5.4 --- NJ AI in Healthcare Transparency Act Data Governance**

**5.4.1 Compliance Obligation.** With respect to data arising from or associated with Meridian\'s three (3) New Jersey facilities (Meridian Atlantic Medical Center, Meridian South Jersey Regional Hospital, and Meridian Princeton Medical Center), Cognitive Foundry shall comply with all data governance provisions of the NJ AI in Healthcare Transparency Act (effective March 1, 2025) (the \"NJ AI Act\"), including without limitation: (a) mandatory documentation of all data inputs used by the ClinIQ™ Platform in connection with AI-assisted clinical decision-making at the New Jersey facilities; (b) maintenance of complete data-provenance records tracing the origin, transformation, and lineage of all data used in AI-assisted clinical decisions; and (c) adherence to the data-retention practices required by the NJ AI Act for records associated with AI-assisted clinical decision-making.

**5.4.2 Regulatory Cooperation.** Cognitive Foundry shall cooperate fully with Meridian in producing any reports, documentation, certifications, or other materials required by the New Jersey Department of Health or any other regulatory authority pursuant to the NJ AI Act. Such cooperation shall include making available to Meridian and to the applicable regulatory authorities the results of independent annual audits conducted by Langford AI Assurance Group, LLC (or another auditor approved by Meridian) pursuant to Exhibit E (AI Model Governance and Audit Terms) to the Agreement, to the extent such results are relevant to NJ AI Act compliance.

**[Section 6 --- Data Retention, Return, and Destruction]{.underline}**

**Section 6.1 --- Retention During Term**

**6.1.1 Retention Limitation.** Cognitive Foundry shall retain Customer Data only for so long as reasonably necessary to perform the services under the Agreement and to comply with its legal, regulatory, and contractual obligations. Cognitive Foundry shall not retain Customer Data beyond the period required for the foregoing purposes.

**6.1.2 Data-Retention Schedule.** Cognitive Foundry shall develop, implement, and maintain a comprehensive data-retention schedule that specifies the maximum retention period for each category of data, organized by data classification tier (as set forth in Section 3.1 of this Exhibit D). The data-retention schedule shall be submitted to Meridian for review and approval within thirty (30) days of the execution of this Exhibit D, and shall be updated at least annually thereafter or upon any material change in applicable legal or regulatory requirements. Cognitive Foundry shall not materially modify the approved data-retention schedule without Meridian\'s prior written consent.

**Section 6.2 --- Return and Destruction Upon Termination or Expiration**
